hadiths.narrator: 'Umar bin Al-Khattab (May Allah be pleased with him), reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "The deeds are considered by the intentions, and a person will get the reward according to his intention. So whoever emigrated for Allah and His Messenger, his emigration will be for Allah and His Messenger; and whoever emigrated for worldly benefits or for a woman to marry, his emigration would be for what he emigrated for". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈЩ…ЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶЩҠЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШӯЩҒШө Ш№Щ…Шұ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®Ш·Ш§ШЁ ШЁЩҶ ЩҶЩҒЩҠЩ„ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШІЩү ШЁЩҶ ШұЩҠШ§Шӯ ШЁЩҶ ЩӮШұШ· ШЁЩҶ ШұШІШ§Шӯ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШҜЩү ШЁЩҶ Щ„ШӨЩү Ш§ШЁЩҶ ШәШ§Щ„ШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШұШҙЩү Ш§Щ„Ш№ШҜЩҲЩүвҖҸ.вҖҸ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШЈШ№Щ…Ш§Щ„ ШЁШ§Щ„ЩҶЩҠШ§ШӘШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ Щ„ЩғЩ„ Ш§Щ…ШұЩүШЎ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶЩҲЩү ЩҒЩ…ЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ЩҮШ¬ШұШӘЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШұШіЩҲЩ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҮШ¬ШұШӘЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШұШіЩҲЩ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ЩҮШ¬ШұШӘЩҮ Щ„ШҜЩҶЩҠШ§ ЩҠШөЩҠШЁЩҮШ§ШҢ ШЈЩҲ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШ© ЩҠЩҶЩғШӯЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҮШ¬ШұШӘЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…Ш§ ЩҮШ§Ш¬Шұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„Щү ШөШӯШӘЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШҘЩ…Ш§Щ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӯШҜШ«ЩҠЩҶвҖҸ: ШЈШЁЩҲ Ш№ШЁШҜШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜ ШЁЩҶ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ШЁЩҶ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШәЩҠШұШ© ШЁЩҶ ШЁЩҺШұЩ’ШҜЩҗШІЩ’ШЁЩҺЩҮЩ’ Ш§Щ„Ш¬Ш№ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠШҢ ШЈШЁЩҲ Ш§Щ„ШӯШіЩҠЩҶ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ… ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӯШ¬Ш§Ш¬ ШЁЩҶ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ЩӮШҙЩҠШұЩү Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҠШіШ§ШЁЩҲШұЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ ШөШӯЩҠШӯЩҮЩ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈШөШӯ Ш§Щ„ЩғШӘШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШөЩҶЩҒШ©вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Narrated 'A'ishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "An army will raid the Ka'bah and when it reaches a desert land, all of them will be swallowed up by the earth." She asked; "O Messenger of Allah! Why all of them?" He answered, "All of them will be swallowed by the earth but they will be raised for Judgement according to their intentions." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶЩҠЩҶ ШЈЩ… Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Ш§ШҰШҙШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҠШәШІЩҲ Ш¬ЩҠШҙ Ш§Щ„ЩғШ№ШЁШ© ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҲШ§ ШЁШЁЩҠШҜШ§ШЎ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ЩҠШ®ШіЩҒ ШЁШЈЩҲЩ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҲШўШ®ШұЩҮЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩғЩҠЩҒ ЩҠШ®ШіЩҒ ШЁШЈЩҲЩ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҲШўШ®ШұЩҮЩ… ЩҲЩҒЩҠЩҮЩ… ШЈШіЩҲШ§ЩӮЩҮЩ… ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ Щ„ЩҠШі Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ…вҖҸ!вҖҸвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҠШ®ШіЩҒ ШЁШЈЩҲЩ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҲШўШ®ШұЩҮЩ…ШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩҠШЁШ№Ш«ЩҲЩҶ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩҶЩҠШ§ШӘЩҮЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Щ„ЩҒШё Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.body: A'ishah (May Allah be pleased with her) narrated that the Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is no emigration after the conquest (of Makkah) but only Jihad [(striving and fighting in the cause of Allah) will continue] and good intention.* So if you are summoned to fight, go forth." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]. .
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш№Ш§ШҰШҙШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ ЩҮШ¬ШұШ© ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш§Щ„ЩҒШӘШӯШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩҶ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ШҜ ЩҲЩҶЩҠШ©ШҢ ЩҲШҘШ°Ш§ Ш§ШіШӘЩҶЩҒШұШӘЩ… ЩҒШ§ЩҶЩҒШұЩҲШ§вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Jabir bin Abdullah Al-Ansari (May Allah be pleasedwith them) reported:
hadiths.body: We accompanied the Prophet (п·ә) in an expedition when he said, "There are some men in Al-Madinah who are with you wherever you march and whichever valley you cross. They have not joined you in person because of their illness." In another version he said: "They share the reward with you." [Muslim]. It is narrated by Bukhari from Anas bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him): We were coming back from the battle of Tabuk with the Prophet (п·ә) when he remarked, "There are people whom we left behind in Al- Madinah who accompanied us in spirit in every pass and valley we crossed. They remained behind for a valid excuse."
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш¬Ш§ШЁШұ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШөШ§ШұЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш№ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІШ§Ш©ЩҚ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ…ШҜЩҠЩҶШ© Щ„ШұШ¬Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢ Щ…Ш§ШіШұШӘЩ… Щ…ШіЩҠШұШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩӮШ·Ш№ШӘЩ… ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШ§ЩӢ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҲШ§ Щ…Ш№ЩғЩ… ШӯШЁШіЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұШ¶вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩ„Ш§ ШҙШ§ШұЩғЩҲЩғЩ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШЈШ¬ШұвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩҲШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ Ш№ЩҶ ШЈЩҶШі ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШұШ¬Ш№ЩҶШ§ Щ…ЩҶ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӘШЁЩҲЩғ Щ…Ш№ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШЈЩӮЩҲШ§Щ…Ш§ЩӢ Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҶШ§ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ…ШҜЩҠЩҶШ© Щ…Ш§ ШіЩ„ЩғЩҶШ§ ШҙШ№ШЁШ§ЩӢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШ§ЩӢ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩҲЩҮЩ… Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ШҢ ШӯШЁШіЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш°ШұвҖҸ"вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ma'n bin Yazid bin Akhnas (May Allah be pleased with them) (he, his father and his grandfather, all were Companions) reported:
hadiths.body: My father set aside some dinars for charity and gave them to a man in the mosque. I went to that man and took back those dinars. He said: "I had not intended you to be given." So we went to Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and put forth the matter before him. He said to my father, "Yazid, you have been rewarded for what you intended." And he said to me, "Ma'n, you are entitled to what you have taken." [Al- Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҠШІЩҠШҜ Щ…Ш№ЩҶ ШЁЩҶ ЩҠШІЩҠШҜ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШ®ЩҶШі ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ЩҲШЈШЁЩҲЩҮ ЩҲШ¬ШҜЩҮ ШөШӯШ§ШЁЩҠЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҠШІЩҠШҜ ШЈШ®ШұШ¬ ШҜЩҶШ§ЩҶЩҠШұ ЩҠШӘШөШҜЩӮ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮШ§ Ш№ЩҶШҜ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜ ЩҒШ¬ШҰШӘ ЩҒШЈШ®Ш°ШӘЩҮШ§ ЩҒШЈШӘЩҠШӘЩҮ ШЁЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШҘЩҠШ§Щғ ШЈШұШҜШӘШҢ ЩҒШ®Ш§ШөЩ…ШӘЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶЩҲЩҠШӘ ЩҠШ§ ЩҠШІЩҠШҜШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щғ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШ®Ш°ШӘ ЩҠШ§Щ…Ш№ЩҶвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Ishaq Sa'd bin Abu Waqqas (May Allah be pleased with him) (one of the ten who had been given the glad tidings of entry into Jannah) narrated:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) visited me in my illness which became severe in the year of Hajjat-ul-Wada' (Farewell Pilgrimage). I said, "O Messenger of Allah, you can see the pain which I am suffering and I am a man of means and there is none to inherit from me except one daughter. Should I give two-thirds of my property in charity?" He (п·ә) said, "No". I asked him, "Then half?" He said, "No". Then I asked, "Can I give away one-third". He said, "Give away one-third, and that is still too much. It is better to leave your heirs well-off than to leave them poor, begging people. You will not expend a thing in charity for the sake of Allah, but you will be rewarded for it; even the morsel of food which you feed your wife". I said, "O Messenger of Allah, would I survive my companions?" He said, "If you survive others and accomplish a thing for the sake of Allah, you would gain higher ranking and standing. You will survive them ... your survival will be beneficial to people (the Muslim) and harmful to others (the enemies of Islam). You will survive others till the people will derive benefit from you, and others would be harmed by you." Messenger of Allah (п·ә) further said, "O Allah, complete for my Companions their emigration and do not cause them to retract." Sa'd bin Khaulah was unfortunate. Messenger of Allah (п·ә) lamented his death as he died in Makkah. [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШҘШіШӯШ§ЩӮ ШіШ№ШҜ ШЁЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҲЩӮШ§Шө Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ ШЁЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩҠШЁ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Щ…ЩҶШ§ЩҒ ШЁЩҶ ШІЩҮШұШ© ШЁЩҶ ЩғЩ„Ш§ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ…ШұШ© ШЁЩҶ ЩғШ№ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ„ШӨЩү Ш§Щ„ЩӮШұШҙ Ш§Щ„ШІЩҮШұЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ШЈШӯШҜ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШҙШұШ© Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҙЩҮЩҲШҜ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҶЩү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ№ЩҲШҜЩҶЩү Ш№Ш§Щ… ШӯШ¬Ш© Ш§Щ„ЩҲШҜШ§Ш№ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҲШ¬Ш№ Ш§ШҙШӘШҜ ШЁЩү ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩҶЩҠ ЩӮШҜ ШЁЩ„Шә ШЁЩү Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ¬Ш№ Щ…Ш§ ШӘШұЩүШҢ ЩҲШЈЩҶШ§ Ш°ЩҲ Щ…Ш§Щ„ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШұШ«ЩҶЩү ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§ШЁЩҶШ© Щ„ЩҠШҢ ШЈЩҒШ§ШӘШөШҜЩӮ ШЁШ«Щ„Ш«Щү Щ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҠвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ШҢ ЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ§Щ„ШҙШ·Шұ ЩҠШ§ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ШҢ ЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш« ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш« ЩҲШ§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш« ЩғШ«ЩҠШұ- ШЈЩҲ ЩғШЁЩҠШұ- ШҘЩҶЩғ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ°Шұ ЩҲШұШ«ШӘЩғ ШЈШәЩҶЩҠШ§ШЎ Ш®ЩҠШұ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ°ШұЩҮЩ… Ш№Ш§Щ„Ш© ЩҠШӘЩғЩҒЩҒЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШіШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩғ Щ„ЩҶ ШӘЩҶЩҒЩӮ ЩҶЩҒЩӮШ© ШӘШЁШӘШәЩү ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШЈШ¬ШұШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ ШӯШӘЩү Щ…Ш§ ШӘШ¬Ш№Щ„ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҒЩҠЩ‘ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩғ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈШ®Щ„ЩҒ ШЁШ№ШҜ ШЈШөШӯШ§ШЁЩҠвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩғ Щ„ЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ ЩҒШӘШ№Щ…Щ„ Ш№Щ…Щ„Ш§ ШӘШЁШӘШәЩҠ ШЁЩҮЩҲШ¬ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§ШІШҜШҜШӘ ШЁЩҮ ШҜШұШ¬Ш© ЩҲШұЩҒШ№Ш©ЩӢШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш№Щ„Щғ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ ШӯШӘЩү ЩҠЩҶШӘЩҒШ№ ШЁЩғ ШЈЩӮЩҲШ§Щ… ЩҲЩҠШ¶ШұЩ‘ ШЁЩғ ШўШ®ШұЩҲЩҶвҖҸ.вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ…Ш¶ Щ„ШўШөШӯШ§ШЁЩү ЩҮШ¬ШұШӘЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұШҜЩҮЩ… Ш№Щ„Щү ШЈШ№ЩӮШ§ШЁЩҮЩ…ШҢ Щ„ЩғЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ§ШҰШі ШіШ№ШҜ ШЁЩҶ Ш®ЩҲЩ„Ш©вҖҸ вҖҸ ЩҠШұШ«Щү Щ„ЩҮ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЈЩҶ Щ…Ш§ШӘ ШЁЩ…ЩғШ©вҖҸ.вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) narrated:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Allah does not look at your figures, nor at your attire but He looks at your hearts and accomplishments". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…ЩҶ ШЁЩҶ ШөШ®Шұ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ
вҖҸ вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈШ¬ШіШ§Щ…ЩғЩ… ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШөЩҲШұЩғЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩҶ ЩҠЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„Щү ЩӮЩ„ЩҲШЁЩғЩ… ЩҲШЈШ№Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩғЩ…вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was asked about who fights in the battlefield out of valour, or out of zeal, or out of hypocrisy, which of this is considered as fighting in the cause of Allah? He said:
hadiths.body: "He who fights in order that the Word of Allah remains the supreme, is considered as fighting in the cause of Allah". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Щ…ЩҲШіЩү Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ ЩӮЩҠШі Ш§Щ„ШЈШҙШ№ШұЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіШҰЩ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҠЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ШҙШ¬Ш§Ш№Ш©ШҢ ЩҲЩҠЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ШӯЩ…ЩҠШ©ЩӢШҢ ЩҲЩҠЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ШұЩҠШ§ШЎШҢ ШЈЩү Ш°Щ„Щғ ЩҒЩҠ ШіШЁЩҠЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ Щ„ШӘЩғЩҲЩҶ ЩғЩ„Щ…Ш© Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҮЩү Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„ЩҠШ§ ЩҒЩҮЩҲ ЩҒЩҠ ШіШЁЩҠЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Bakrah Ath-Thaqafi (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said: "When two Muslims are engaged in a combat against each other with their sword's and one is killed, both are doomed to Hell". I said, "O Messenger of Allah! As to the one who kills, it is understandable, but why the slain one?" He (п·ә) replied: "He was eager to kill his opponent". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШЁЩғШұШ© ЩҶЩҒЩҠШ№ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӯШ§ШұШ« Ш§Щ„Ш«ЩӮЩҒЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘШ° Ш§Щ„ШӘЩӮЩү Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…Ш§ЩҶ ШЁШіЩҠЩҒЩҠЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҒШ§Щ„ЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…ЩӮШӘЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШұвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮЩ„ШӘ ЩҠШ§ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ ШЁШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩӮШӘЩҲЩ„вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШӯШұЩҠШөШ§ЩӢ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩӮШӘЩ„ ШөШ§ШӯШЁЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported that:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: "The reward for Salat performed by a person in congregation is more than 20 times greater than that of the Salat performed in one's house or shop. When one performs Wudu' perfectly and then proceeds to the mosque with the sole intention of performing Salat, then for every step he takes towards the mosque, he is upgraded one degree in reward and one of his sins is eliminated until he enters the mosque, and when he enters the mosque, he is considered as performing Salat as long as it is the Salat which prevents him (from leaving the mosque); and the angels keep on supplicating Allah for him as long as he remains in his place of prayer. They say: 'O Allah! have mercy on him; O Allah! forgive his sins; O Allah! accept his repentance'. This will carry on as long as he does not pass wind". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШөЩ„Ш§Ш© Ш§Щ„ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҒЩҠ Ш¬Щ…Ш§Ш№Ш© ШӘШІЩҠШҜ Ш№Щ„Щү ШөЩ„Ш§ШӘЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩҲЩӮЩҮ ЩҲШЁЩҠШӘЩҮ ШЁШ¶Ш№Ш§ЩӢ ЩҲШ№ШҙШұЩҠЩҶ ШҜШұШ¬ЩҮ ЩҲШ°Щ„Щғ ШЈЩҶ ШЈШӯШҜЩҮЩ… ШҘШ°Ш§ ШӘЩҲШ¶ШЈ ЩҒШЈШӯШіЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ¶ЩҲШЎ Ш«Щ… ШЈШӘЩү Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШұЩҠШҜ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш©ШҢ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҶЩҮШІЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш©ШҢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШ®Ш· Ш®Ш·ЩҲШ© ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШұЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҮШ§ ШҜШұШ¬Ш©ШҢ ЩҲШӯШ· Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЁЩҮШ§ Ш®Ш·ЩҠШҰШ© ШӯШӘЩү ЩҠШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© ЩҮЩү ШӘШӯШЁШіЩҮШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© ЩҠШөЩ„ЩҲЩҶ Ш№Щ„Щү ШЈШӯШҜЩғЩ… Щ…Ш§ ШҜШ§Щ… ЩҒЩҠ Щ…Ш¬Щ„ШіЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш°Щү ШөЩ„Щү ЩҒЩҠЩҮШҢ Щ…Ш§ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШӯШҜШ« ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩҮШ°Ш§ Щ„ЩҒШё Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ 'вҖҸвҖҸ вҖҸЩҠЩҶЩҮШІЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ ЩҮЩҲ ШЁЩҒШӘШӯ Ш§Щ„ЩҠШ§ШЎ ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩҮШ§ШЎ ЩҲШЁШ§Щ„ШІШ§ЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩү ЩҠШ®ШұШ¬ЩҮ ЩҲЩҠЩҶЩҮШ¶ЩҮ вҖҸ'

hadiths.narrator: 'Abdullah bin 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said that Allah, the Glorious, said: "Verily, Allah (SWT) has ordered that the good and the bad deeds be written down. Then He explained it clearly how (to write): He who intends to do a good deed but he does not do it, then Allah records it for him as a full good deed, but if he carries out his intention, then Allah the Exalted, writes it down for him as from ten to seven hundred folds, and even more. But if he intends to do an evil act and has not done it, then Allah writes it down with Him as a full good deed, but if he intends it and has done it, Allah writes it down as one bad deed". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШЁШ§Ші Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш·Щ„ШЁ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ШҢ Ш№ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩ…Ш§ ЩҠШұЩҲЩү Ш№ЩҶ ШұШЁЩҮШҢ ШӘШЁШ§ШұЩғ ЩҲШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩғШӘШЁ Ш§Щ„ШӯШіЩҶШ§ШӘ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШіЩҠШҰШ§ШӘ Ш«Щ… ШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш°Щ„ЩғвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩ…ЩҶ ЩҮЩ…Щ‘ ШЁШӯШіЩҶШ© ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ№Щ…Щ„ЩҮШ§ ЩғШӘШЁЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШЁШ§ШұЩғ ЩҲШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӯШіЩҶШ© ЩғШ§Щ…Щ„Ш©ШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ЩҮЩ… ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҒШ№Щ…Щ„ЩҮШ§ ЩғШӘШЁЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШҙШұ ШӯШіЩҶШ§ШӘ ШҘЩ„Щү ШіШЁШ№Щ…Ш§ШҰЩҮ Ш¶Ш№ЩҒ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈШ¶Ш№Ш§ЩҒ ЩғШ«ЩҠШұШ©ШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ЩҮЩ… ШЁШіЩҠШҰШ© ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ№Щ…Щ„ЩҮШ§ ЩғШӘШЁЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮ ШӯШіЩҶШ© ЩғШ§Щ…Щ„Ш©ШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ЩҮЩ…Щ‘ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҒШ№Щ…Щ„ЩҮШ§ ЩғШӘШЁЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШіЩҠШҰШ© ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜШ© вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: 'Abdullah bin 'Umar bin Al-Khattab (May Allah be pleased with them) narrated that:
hadiths.body: He heard Messenger of Allah (п·ә) as saying: "Three men, amongst those who came before you, set out until night came and they reached a cave, so they entered it. A rock fell down from the mountain and blocked the entrance of the cave. They said: 'Nothing will save you from this unless you supplicate to Allah by virtue of a righteous deed you have done.' Thereupon, one of them said: 'O Allah! I had parents who were old, and I used to offer them milk before any of my children or slaves. One day, I went far away in search of grazing and could not come back until they had slept. When I milked as usual and brought the drink I found them both asleep. I hated to disturb them and also disliked to give milk to my children before them. My children were crying out of hunger at my feet but I awaited with the bowl in my hand for them to wake up. When they awoke at dawn, they drank milk. O Allah! If I did so to seek Your Pleasure, then deliver us from the distress caused by the rock'. The rock moved slightly but they were unable to escape. The next said: 'O Allah! I had a cousin whom I loved more than any one else (in another version he said: as a man can love a woman). I wanted to have sexual intercourse with her but she refused. Hard pressed in a year of famine, she approached me. I gave her one hundred and twenty dinars on condition that she would yield herself to me. She agreed and when we got together (for sexual intercourse), she said: Fear Allah and do not break the seal unlawfully. I moved away from her in spite of the fact that I loved her most passionately; and I let her keep the money I had given her. O Allah! If I did that to seek Your Pleasure, then, remove the distress in which we are.' The rock moved aside a bit further but they were still unable to get out. The third one said: 'O Allah! I hired some labourers and paid them their wages except one of them departed without taking his due. I invested his money in business and the business prospered greatly. After a long time, he came to me and said: O slave of Allah! Pay me my dues. I said: All that you see is yours - camels, cattle, goats and slaves. He said: O slave of Allah! Do not mock at me. I assured him that I was not joking. So he took all the things and went away. He spared nothing. O Allah! If I did so seeking Your Pleasure, then relieve us of our distress.' The rock slipped aside and they got out walking freely". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…ЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…Шұ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®Ш·Ш§ШЁШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸ Ш§ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮ Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© ЩҶЩҒШұ Щ…Щ…ЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩғЩ… ШӯШӘЩү ШўЩҲШ§ЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…ШЁЩҠШӘ ШҘЩ„Щү ШәШ§Шұ ЩҒШҜШ®Щ„ЩҲЩҮШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶШӯШҜШұШӘ ШөШ®ШұШ© Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШЁЩ„ ЩҒШіШҜШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШәШ§ШұШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҶШ¬ЩҠЩғЩ… Щ…ЩҶ ЩҮШ°ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШөШ®ШұШ© ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШҜШ№ЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁШөШ§Щ„Шӯ ШЈШ№Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩғЩ…вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШ¬Щ„ Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩғШ§ЩҶ Щ„ЩҠ ШЈШЁЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҠШ®Ш§ЩҶ ЩғШЁЩҠШұШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩғЩҶШӘ Щ„Ш§ ШЈШәШЁЩӮ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҮЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩҶШЈЩү ШЁЩү Ш·Щ„ШЁ Ш§Щ„ШҙШ¬Шұ ЩҠЩҲЩ…Ш§ЩӢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ШЈШұШӯ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШӯШӘЩү ЩҶШ§Щ…Ш§ ЩҒШӯЩ„ШЁШӘ Щ„ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШәШЁЩҲЩӮЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҒЩҲШ¬ШҜШӘЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҶШ§ШҰЩ…ЩҠЩҶ ЩҒЩғШұЩҮШӘ ШЈЩҶ ШЈЩҲЩӮШёЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҲШЈЩҶ ШЈШәШЁЩӮ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҮЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ШЈЩҲ Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩ„ШЁШ«ШӘ- ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩӮШҜШӯ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩҠШҜЩү- ШЈЩҶШӘШёШұ Ш§ШіШӘЩҠЩӮШ§ШёЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШӯШӘЩү ШЁШұЩӮ Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ¬Шұ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШөШЁЩҠШ© ЩҠШӘШ¶Ш§ШәЩҲЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜ ЩӮШҜЩ…Щү- ЩҒШ§ШіШӘЩҠЩӮШёШ§ ЩҒШҙШұШЁШ§ ШәШЁЩҲЩӮЩҮЩ…Ш§вҖҸ.вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШҘЩҶ ЩғЩҶШӘ ЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш§ШЁШӘШәШ§ШЎ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩғ ЩҒЩҒШұШ¬ Ш№ЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҮШ°ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШөШ®ШұШ©ШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶЩҒШұШ¬ШӘ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШіШӘШ·ЩҠШ№ЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШұЩҲШ¬ Щ…ЩҶЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШўШ®ШұвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ Щ„ЩҠ Ш§ШЁЩҶШ© Ш№Щ… ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ШЈШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШҘЩ„ЩүЩ‘ вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩғЩҶШӘ ШЈШӯШЁЩҮШ§ ЩғШЈШҙШҜ Щ…Ш§ ЩҠШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„ШұШ¬Ш§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШіШ§ШЎШҢ ЩҒШЈШұШҜШӘЩҮШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩҶЩҒШіЩҮШ§ ЩҒШ§Щ…ШӘЩҶШ№ШӘ Щ…ЩҶЩү ШӯШӘЩү ШЈЩ„Щ…Щ‘ШӘ ШЁЩҮШ§ ШіЩҶШ© Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҶЩҠЩҶ ЩҒШ¬Ш§ШЎШӘЩҶЩү ЩҒШЈШ№Ш·ЩҠШӘЩҮШ§ Ш№ШҙШұЩҠЩҶ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ШҰШ© ШҜЩҠЩҶШ§Шұ Ш№Щ„Щү ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„Щү ШЁЩҠЩҶЩү ЩҲШЁЩҠЩҶ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩӮШҜШұШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ЩӮШ№ШҜШӘ ШЁЩҠЩҶ ШұШ¬Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§ШӘЩӮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҒШ¶ Ш§Щ„Ш®Ш§ШӘЩ… ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШЁШӯЩӮЩҮШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶШөШұЩҒШӘ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩҲЩҮЩү ШЈШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШҘЩ„Щү ЩҲШӘШұЩғШӘ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҮШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш°Щү ШЈШ№Ш·ЩҠШӘЩҮШ§ШҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШҘЩҶ ЩғЩҶШӘ ЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш§ШЁШӘШәШ§ШЎ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩғ ЩҒШ§ЩҒШұШ¬ Ш№ЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶЩҒШұШ¬ШӘ Ш§Щ„ШөШ®ШұШ© ШәЩҠШұ ШЈЩҶЩҮЩ… Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШіШӘШ·ЩҠШ№ЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШұЩҲШ¬ Щ…ЩҶЩҮШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш§Щ„Ш«вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… Ш§ШіШӘШЈШ¬ШұШӘ ШЈШ¬ШұШ§ШЎ ЩҲШЈШ№Ш·ЩҠШӘЩҮЩ… ШЈШ¬ШұЩҮЩ… ШәЩҠШұ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜ ШӘШұЩғ Ш§Щ„Ш°Щү Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ°ЩҮШЁШҢ ЩҒШ«Щ…ШұШӘ ШЈШ¬ШұЩҮ ШӯШӘЩү ЩғШ«ШұШӘ Щ…ЩҶЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ…ЩҲШ§Щ„ШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш§ШЎЩҶЩү ШЁШ№ШҜ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈШҜЩ‘ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈШ¬ШұЩүШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩ„ Щ…Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШ¬ШұЩғвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШҘШЁЩ„ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШЁЩӮШұ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШәЩҶЩ… ЩҲШ§Щ„ШұЩӮЩҠЩӮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ШӘШіШӘЩҮШІШҰ ШЁЩүвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ ШЈШіШӘЩҮШІШҰ ШЁЩғШҢ ЩҒШЈШ®Ш°ЩҮ ЩғЩ„ЩҮ ЩҒШ§ШіШӘШ§ЩӮЩҮ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШӘШұЩғ Щ…ЩҶЩҮ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢШҢ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШҘЩҶ ЩғЩҶШӘЩҸ ЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш§ШЁШӘШәШ§ШЎ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩғ ЩҒШ§ЩҒШұШ¬ Ш№ЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶЩҒШұШ¬ШӘ Ш§Щ„ШөШ®ШұШ© ЩҒШ®ШұШ¬ЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩ…ШҙЩҲЩҶвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: I heard Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying: "By Allah, I seek Allah's forgiveness and repent to Him more than seventy times a day." [Al-Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩҶЩҠ Щ„ШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШЈШӘЩҲШЁ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… ШЈЩғШ«Шұ Щ…ЩҶ ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ Щ…ШұШ© вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Al-Agharr bin Yasar Al-Muzani (May Allah be pleased with him) narrated that:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: "Turn you people in repentance to Allah and beg pardon of Him. I turn to Him in repentance a hundred times a day". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШәШұ ШЁЩҶ ЩҠШіШ§Шұ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШІЩҶЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ШЈЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШӘЩҲШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ§ШіШӘШәЩҒШұЩҲЩҮ ЩҒШҘЩҶЩү ШЈШӘЩҲШЁ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… Щ…Ш§ШҰЩҮ Щ…ШұШ©вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Anas bin Malik Al-Ansari (MayAllah be pleased with him) the servant of the Messenger of Allah narrated:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Verily, Allah is more delighted with the repentance of His slave than a person who lost his camel in a desert land and then finds it (unexpectedly)". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]. In another version of Muslim, he said: "Verily, Allah is more pleased with the repentance of His slave than a person who has his camel in a waterless desert carrying his provision of food and drink and it is lost. He, having lost all hopes (to get that back), lies down in shade and is disappointed about his camel; when all of a sudden he finds that camel standing before him. He takes hold of its reins and then out of boundless joy blurts out: 'O Allah, You are my slave and I am Your Rubb'.He commits this mistake out of extreme joy".
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШӯЩ…ШІШ© ШЈЩҶШі ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШөШ§ШұЩү Ш®Ш§ШҜЩ… ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…ШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩҒШұШӯ ШЁШӘЩҲШЁШ© Ш№ШЁШҜЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШӯШҜЩғЩ… ШіЩӮШ· Ш№Щ„Щү ШЁШ№ЩҠШұЩҮ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШЈШ¶Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ШЈШұШ¶ ЩҒЩ„Ш§Ш© вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈШҙШҜ ЩҒШұШӯШ§ ШЁШӘЩҲШЁШ© Ш№ШЁШҜЩҮ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ЩҠШӘЩҲШЁ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШӯШҜЩғЩ… ЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш№Щ„Щү ШұШ§ШӯЩ„ШӘЩҮ ШЁШЈШұШ¶ ЩҒЩ„Ш§Ш©ШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶЩҒЩ„ШӘШӘ Щ…ЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШ№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ…ЩҮ ЩҲШҙШұШ§ШЁЩҮ ЩҒШЈЩҠШі Щ…ЩҶЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒШЈШӘЩү ШҙШ¬ШұШ© ЩҒШ§Ш¶Ш·Ш¬Ш№ ЩҒЩҠ ШёЩ„ЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШЈЩҠШі Щ…ЩҶ ШұШ§ШӯЩ„ШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҒШЁЩҠЩҶЩ…Ш§ ЩҮЩҲ ЩғШ°Щ„Щғ ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҮЩҲ ШЁЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩӮШ§ШҰЩ…Ш© Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮ ШҢ ЩҒШЈШ®Ш° ШЁШ®Ш·Ш§Щ…ЩҮШ§ Ш«Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ ШҙШҜШ© Ш§Щ„ЩҒШұШӯвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЈЩҶШӘ Ш№ШЁШҜЩҠ ЩҲШЈЩҶШ§ ШұШЁЩғШҢ ШЈШ®Ш·ШЈ Щ…ЩҶ ШҙШҜШ© Ш§Щ„ЩҒШұШӯвҖҸ"вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Musa Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said: "Allah, the Exalted, will continue to stretch out His Hand in the night so that the sinners of the day may repent, and continue to stretch His Hand in the daytime so that the sinners of the night may repent, until the sun rises from the west". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Щ…ЩҲШіЩү Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ ЩӮЩҠШі Ш§Щ„ШЈШҙШ№ШұЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҠШЁШіШ· ЩҠШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„ Щ„ЩҠШӘЩҲШЁ Щ…ШіЩҠШЎ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҮШ§ШұШҢ ЩҲЩҠШЁШіШ· ЩҠШҜЩҮ ШЁШ§Щ„ЩҶЩҮШ§Шұ Щ„ЩҠШӘЩҲШЁ Щ…ШіЩҠШЎ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„ ШӯШӘЩү ШӘШ·Щ„Ш№ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…Ші Щ…ЩҶ Щ…ШәШұШЁЩҮШ§вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) narrated:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "He who repents before the sun rises from the west, Allah will forgive him". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘШ§ШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ·Щ„Ш№ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…Ші Щ…ЩҶ Щ…ШәШұШЁЩҮШ§ ШӘШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: 'Abdullah bin 'Umar bin Al-Khattab (May Allah be pleased with them) reported that:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "Allah accepts a slave's repentance as long as the latter is not on his death bed (that is, before the soul of the dying person reaches the throat)". [At-Tirmidhi, who categorised it as Hadith Hasan].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…ЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…Шұ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®Ш·Ш§ШЁ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ ЩҠЩӮШЁЩ„ ШӘЩҲШЁШ© Ш§Щ„Ш№ШЁШҜ Щ…Ш§ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШәШұШәШұвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Zirr bin Hubaish reported:
hadiths.body: I went to Safwan bin 'Assal (May Allah be pleased with him) to inquire about wiping with wet hands over light boots while performing Wudu'. He asked me, "What brings you here, Zirr?" I answered: "Search for knowledge". He said, "Angels spread their wings for the seeker of knowledge out of joy for what he seeks". I told him, "I have some doubts in my mind regarding wiping of wet hands over light boots in the course of performing Wudu' after defecation or urinating. Now since you are one of the Companions of the Prophet (п·ә), I have come to ask you whether you heard any saying of the Prophet (п·ә) concerning it?". He replied in the affirmative and said, "He (п·ә) instructed us that during a journey we need not take off our light boots for washing the feet up to three days and nights, except in case of major impurity (after sexual intercourse). In other cases such as sleeping, relieving oneself or urinating, the wiping of wet hands over light boots will suffice." I, then, questioned him, "Did you hear him say anything about love and affection?" He replied, "We accompanied the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) in a journey when a bedouin called out in a loud voice, 'O Muhammad.' The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) replied him in the same tone, 'Here I am.' I said to him (the bedouin), 'Woe to you, lower your voice in his presence, because you are not allowed to do so.' He said, 'By Allah! I will not lower my voice,' and then addressing the Prophet (п·ә) he said, 'What about a person who loves people but has not found himself in their company.' Messenger of Allah (п·ә) replied, 'On the Day of Resurrection, a person will be in the company of those whom he loves.' The Messenger of Allah then kept on talking to us and in the course of his talk, he mentioned a gateway in the heaven, the width of which could be crossed by a rider in forty or seventy years". Sufyan, one of the narrators of this tradition, said: "This gateway is in the direction of Syria. Allah created it on the day He created the heavens and the earth. It is open for repentance and will not be shut until the sun rises from that direction (i.e., the West) (on Doomsday)". [At-Tirmidhi, who categorised it as Hadith Hasan Sahih]
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШІШұ ШЁЩҶ ШӯШЁЩҠШҙ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШӘЩҠШӘ ШөЩҒЩҲШ§ЩҶ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШіШ§Щ„ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈШіШЈЩ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШӯ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Ш®ЩҒЩҠЩҶ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…Ш§ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШЁЩғ ЩҠШ§ШІШұвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§ШЁШӘШәШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„Щ…ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© ШӘШ¶Ш№ ШЈШ¬ЩҶШӯШӘЩҮШ§ Щ„Ш·Ш§Щ„ШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„Щ… ШұШ¶ЩҠ ШЁЩ…Ш§ ЩҠШ·Щ„ШЁШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШөШӯШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҢ ЩҒШ¬ШҰШӘ ШЈШіШЈЩ„ЩғвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩ„ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘЩҮ ЩҠШ°ЩғШұ ЩҒЩҠ Ш°Щ„Щғ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҠШЈЩ…ШұЩҶШ§ ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩғЩҶШ§ ШіЩҒШұШ§ЩӢ- ШЈЩҲ Щ…ШіШ§ЩҒШұЩҠЩҶ- ШЈЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ЩҶЩҶШІШ№ Ш®ЩҒШ§ЩҒЩҶШ§ЩӢ Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© ШЈЩҠШ§Щ… ЩҲЩ„ЩҠШ§Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩҶ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш¬ЩҶШ§ШЁШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШәШ§ШҰШ· ЩҲШЁЩҲЩ„ ЩҲЩҶЩҲЩ…вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩҒШұШҢ ЩҒШЁЩҠЩҶШ§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮ ШҘШ° ЩҶШ§ШҜШ§ЩҮ ШЈШ№ШұШ§ШЁЩү ШЁШөЩҲШӘ Щ„ЩҮ Ш¬ЩҮЩҲШұЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜШҢ ЩҒШЈШ¬Ш§ШЁЩҮ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҶШӯЩҲШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ ШөЩҲШӘЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҮШ§ШӨЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲЩҠШӯЩғ Ш§ШәШ¶Ш¶ Щ…ЩҶ ШөЩҲШӘЩғ ЩҒШҘЩҶЩғ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҢ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ЩҶЩҮЩҠШӘ Ш№ЩҶ ЩҮШ°Ш§вҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ШЈШәШ¶Ш¶вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈШ№ШұШ§ШЁЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұШЎ ЩҠШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҲЩ… ЩҲЩ„Щ…Ш§ ЩҠЩ„ШӯЩӮ ШЁЩҮЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұШЎ Щ…Ш№ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШӯШЁ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҠШ§Щ…Ш©вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ ШІШ§Щ„ ЩҠШӯШҜШ«ЩҶШ§ ШӯШӘЩү Ш°ЩғШұ ШЁШ§ШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШәШұШЁ Щ…ШіЩҠШұШ© Ш№ШұШ¶ЩҮ ШЈЩҲ ЩҠШіЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„ШұШ§ЩғШЁ ЩҒЩҠ Ш№ШұШ¶ЩҮ ШЈШұШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ ШЈЩҲ ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ Ш№Ш§Щ…Ш§ЩӢвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШіЩҒЩҠШ§ЩҶ ШЈШӯШҜ Ш§Щ„ШұЩҲШ§Ш© ЩӮШЁЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШҙШ§Щ… Ш®Щ„ЩӮЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш®Щ„ЩӮ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ЩҲШ§ШӘ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ Щ…ЩҒШӘЩҲШӯШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ„ШӘЩҲШЁШ© Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШәЩ„ЩӮ ШӯШӘЩү ШӘШ·Щ„Ш№ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…Ші Щ…ЩҶЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠ ЩҲШәЩҠШұЩҮ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶ ШөШӯЩҠШӯвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: Prophet (п·ә) of Allah (п·ә) said: "There was a man from among a nation before you who killed ninety-nine people and then made an inquiry about the most learned person on the earth. He was directed to a monk. He came to him and told him that he had killed ninety-nine people and asked him if there was any chance for his repentance to be accepted. He replied in the negative and the man killed him also completing one hundred. He then asked about the most learned man in the earth. He was directed to a scholar. He told him that he had killed one hundred people and asked him if there was any chance for his repentance to be accepted. He replied in the affirmative and asked, 'Who stands between you and repentance? Go to such and such land; there (you will find) people devoted to prayer and worship of Allah, join them in worship, and do not come back to your land because it is an evil place.' So he went away and hardly had he covered half the distance when death overtook him; and there was a dispute between the angels of mercy and the angels of torment. The angels of mercy pleaded, 'This man has come with a repenting heart to Allah,' and the angels of punishment argued, 'He never did a virtuous deed in his life.' Then there appeared another angel in the form of a human being and the contending angels agreed to make him arbiter between them. He said, 'Measure the distance between the two lands. He will be considered belonging to the land to which he is nearer.' They measured and found him closer to the land (land of piety) where he intended to go, and so the angels of mercy collected his soul". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ ШіШ№ШҜ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ ШЁЩҶ ШіЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШҜШұЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ЩҶШЁЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩ…ЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩғЩ… ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩӮШӘЩ„ ШӘШіШ№Ш© ЩҲШӘШіШ№ЩҠЩҶ ЩҶЩҒШіШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒШіШЈЩ„ Ш№ЩҶ ШЈШ№Щ„Щ… ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ШҢ ЩҒШҜЩ„ Ш№Щ„Щү ШұШ§ЩҮШЁШҢ ЩҒШЈШӘШ§ЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШӘЩ„ ШӘШіШ№ЩҮ ЩҲШӘШіШ№ЩҠЩҶ ЩҶЩҒШіШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩҮЩ„ Щ„ЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘЩҲШЁШ©вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШӘЩ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩғЩ…Щ„ ШЁЩҮ Щ…Ш§ШҰШ©ЩӢШҢ Ш«Щ… ШіШЈЩ„ Ш№ЩҶ ШЈШ№Щ„Щ… ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ШҢ ЩҒШҜЩ„ Ш№Щ„Щү ШұШ¬Щ„ Ш№Ш§Щ„Щ… ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШӘЩ„ Щ…Ш§ШҰШ© ЩҶЩҒШі ЩҒЩҮЩ„ Щ„ЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘЩҲШЁШ©вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩҠШӯЩҲЩ„ ШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҲШЁШ©вҖҸШҹвҖҸ Ш§ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈШұШ¶ ЩғШ°Ш§ ЩҲЩғШ°Ш§ШҢ ЩҒШҘЩҶ ШЁЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЩӢ ЩҠШ№ШЁШҜЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҒШ§Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш№ЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұШ¬Ш№ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈШұШ¶Щғ ЩҒШҘЩҶЩҮШ§ ШЈШұШ¶ ШіЩҲШЎЩҚШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҶШөЩҒ Ш§Щ„Ш·ШұЩҠЩӮ ШЈШӘШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҲШӘШҢ ЩҒШ§Ш®ШӘШөЩ…ШӘ ЩҒЩҠЩҮ Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…Ш© ЩҲЩ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш°Ш§ШЁвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…Ш©вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШӘШ§ШҰШЁШ§ Щ…ЩӮШЁЩ„Ш§ ШЁЩӮЩ„ШЁЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүШҢ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш°Ш§ШЁвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩҮ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШ№Щ…Щ„ Ш®ЩҠШұШ§ ЩӮШ·ШҢ ЩҒШЈШӘШ§ЩҮЩ… Щ…Щ„Щғ ЩҒЩҠ ШөЩҲШұШ© ШўШҜЩ…ЩҠ ЩҒШ¬Ш№Щ„ЩҲЩҮ ШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮЩ…- ШЈЩҠ ШӯЩғЩ…Ш§ЩӢ- ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮЩҠШіЩҲШ§ Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ЩҠЩҶ ЩҒШҘЩ„Щү ШЈЩҠШӘЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШЈШҜЩҶЩү ЩҒЩҮЩҲ Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§ШіЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҲШ¬ШҜЩҲЩҮ ШЈШҜЩҶЩү ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҠ ШЈШұШ§ШҜШҢ ЩҒЩӮШЁШ¶ШӘЩҮ Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ© Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…Ш©вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abdullah bin Ka'b, who served as the guide of Ka'b bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him) when he became blind, narrated:
hadiths.body: I heard Ka'b bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him) narrating the story of his remaining behind instead of joining Messenger of Allah (п·ә) when he left for the battle of Tabuk. Ka'b said: "I accompanied Messenger of Allah (п·ә) in every expedition which he undertook excepting the battle of Tabuk and the battle of Badr. As for the battle of Badr, nobody was blamed for remaining behind as Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and the Muslims, when they set out, had in mind only to intercept the caravan of the Quraish. Allah made them confront their enemies unexpectedly. I had the honour of being with Messenger of Allah (п·ә) on the night of 'Aqabah when we pledged our allegiance to Islam and it was dearer to me than participating in the battle of Badr, although Badr was more well-known among the people than that. And this is the account of my staying behind from the battle of Tabuk. I never had better means and more favourable circumstances than at the time of this expedition. And by Allah, I had never before possessed two riding-camels as I did during the time of this expedition. Whenever Messenger of Allah (п·ә) decided to go on a campaign, he would not disclose his real destination till the last moment (of departure). But on this expedition, he set out in extremely hot weather; the journey was long and the terrain was waterless desert; and he had to face a strong army, so he informed the Muslims about the actual position so that they should make full preparation for the campaign. And the Muslims who accompanied Messenger of Allah (п·ә) at that time were in large number but no proper record of them was maintained." Ka'b (further) said: "Few were the persons who chose to remain absent believing that they could easily hide themselves (and thus remain undetected) unless Revelation from Allah, the Exalted, and Glorious (revealed relating to them). And Messenger of Allah (п·ә) set out on this expedition when the fruit were ripe and their shade was sought. I had a weakness for them and it was during this season that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and the Muslims made preparations. I also would set out in the morning to make preparations along with them but would come back having done nothing and said to myself: 'I have means enough (to make preparations) as soon as I like'. And I went on doing this (postponing my preparations) till the time of departure came and it was in the morning that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) set out along with the Muslims, but I had made no preparations. I would go early in the morning and come back, but with no decision. I went on doing so until they (the Muslims) hastened and covered a good deal of distance. Then I wished to march on and join them. Would that I had done that! But perhaps it was not destined for me. After the departure of Messenger of Allah (п·ә) whenever I went out, I was grieved to find no good example to follow but confirmed hypocrites or weak people whom Allah had exempted (from marching forth for Jihad). Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made no mention of me until he reached Tabuk. While he was sitting with the people in Tabuk, he said, 'What happened to Ka'b bin Malik?' A person from Banu Salimah said: "O Messenger of Allah, the (beauty) of his cloak and an appreciation of his finery have detained him.' Upon this Mu'adh bin Jabal (MatAllah be pleased with him) admonished him and said to Messenger of Allah (п·ә): "By Allah, we know nothing about him but good.' Messenger of Allah (п·ә), however, kept quiet. At that time he (the Prophet (п·ә)) saw a person dressed in white and said, 'Be Abu Khaithamah.' And was Abu Khaithamah Al- Ansari was the person who had contributed a Sa' of dates and was ridiculed by the hypocrites." Ka'b bin Malik further said: "When the news reached me that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was on his way back from Tabuk, I was greatly distressed. I thought of fabricating an excuse and asked myself how I would save myself from his anger the next day. In this connection, I sought the counsels of every prudent member of my family. When I was told that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was about to arrive, all the wicked ideas vanished (from my mind) and I came to the conclusion that nothing but the truth could save me. So I decided to tell him the truth. It was in the morning that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) arrived in Al-Madinah. It was his habit that whenever he came back from a journey, he would first go to the mosque and perform two Rak'ah (of optional prayer) and would then sit with the people. When he sat, those who had remained behind him began to put forward their excuses and take an oath before him. They were more than eighty in number. Messenger of Allah (п·ә) accepted their excuses on the very face of them and accepted their allegiance and sought forgiveness for them and left their insights to Allah, until I appeared before him. I greeted him and he smiled and there was a tinge of anger in that. He then said to me, 'Come forward'. I went forward and I sat in front of him. He said to me, 'What kept you back? Could you not afford to go in for a ride?' I said, 'O Messenger of Allah, by Allah, if I were to sit before anybody else, a man of the world, I would have definitely saved myself from his anger on one pretext or the other and I have a gifted skill in argumentation, but, by Allah, I am fully aware that if I were to put forward before you a lame excuse to please you, Allah would definitely provoke your wrath upon me. In case, I speak the truth, you may be angry with me, but I hope that Allah would be pleased with me (and accept my repentance). By Allah, there is no valid excuse for me. By Allah, I never possessed so good means, and I never had such favourable conditions for me as I had when I stayed behind.' Thereupon, Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, 'This man spoke the truth, so get up (and wait) until Allah gives a decision about you.' I left and some people of Banu Salimah followed me. They said to me, 'By Allah, we do not know that you committed a sin before. You, however, showed inability to put forward an excuse before Messenger of Allah (п·ә) like those who stayed behind him. It would have been enough for the forgiveness of your sin that Messenger of Allah (п·ә) would have sought forgiveness for you.' By Allah, they kept on reproaching me until I thought of going back to Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and retract my confession. Then I said to them, 'Has anyone else met the same fate?' They said, 'Yes, two persons have met the same fate. They made the same statement as you did and the same verdict was delivered in their case.' I asked, 'Who are they?' They said, 'Murarah bin Ar-Rabi' Al-'Amri and Hilal bin Umaiyyah Al- Waqifi.' They mentioned these two pious men who had taken part in the battle of Badr and there was an example for me in them. I was confirmed in my original resolve. Messenger of Allah (п·ә) prohibited the Muslims to talk to the three of us from amongst those who had stayed behind. The people began to avoid us and their attitude towards us changed and it seemed as if the whole atmosphere had turned against us, and it was in fact the same atmosphere of which I was fully aware and in which I had lived (for a fairly long time). We spent fifty nights in this very state and my two friends confined themselves within their houses and spent (most of their) time weeping. As I was the youngest and the strongest, I would leave my house, attend the congregational Salat, move about in the bazaars, but none would speak to me. I would come to Messenger of Allah (п·ә) as he sat amongst (people) after the Salat, greet him and would ask myself whether or not his lips moved in response to my greetings. Then I would perform Salat near him and look at him stealthily. When I finish my Salat, he would look at me and when I would cast a glance at him he would turn away his eyes from me. When the harsh treatment of the Muslims to me continued for a (considerable) length of time, I walked and I climbed upon the wall of the garden of Abu Qatadah, who was my cousin, and I had a great love for him. I greeted him but, by Allah, he did not answer to my greeting. I said to him, 'O Abu Qatadah, I adjure you in the Name of Allah, are you not aware that I love Allah and His Messenger (п·ә)?' I asked him the same question again but he remained silent. I again adjured him, whereupon he said, 'Allah and His Messenger (п·ә) know better.' My eyes were filled with tears, and I came back climbing down the wall. As I was walking in the bazaars of Al-Madinah, a man from the Syrian peasants, who had come to sell food grains in Al-Madinah, asked people to direct him to Ka'b bin Malik. People pointed towards me. He came to me and delivered a letter from the King of Ghassan, and as I was a scribe, I read that letter whose purport was: 'It has been conveyed to us that your friend (the Prophet (п·ә)) was treating you harshly. Allah has not created you for a place where you are to be degraded and where you cannot find your right place; so come to us and we shall receive you graciously.' As I read that letter I said: 'This is too a trial,' so I put it to fire in an oven. When forty days had elapsed and Messenger of Allah (п·ә) received no Revelation, there came to me a messenger of the Messenger of Allah and said, 'Verily, Messenger of Allah (п·ә) has commanded you to keep away from your wife.' I said, 'Should I divorce her or what else should I do?' He said, 'No, but only keep away from her and don't have sexual contact with her.' The same message was sent to my companions. So, I said to my wife: 'You better go to your parents and stay there with them until Allah gives the decision in my case.' The wife of Hilal bin Umaiyyah came to Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, Hilal bin Umaiyyah is a senile person and has no servant. Do you disapprove if I serve him?' He said, 'No, but don't let him have any sexual contact with you.' She said, 'By Allah, he has no such desire left in him. By Allah, he has been in tears since (this calamity) struck him.' Members of my family said to me, 'You should have sought permission from Messenger of Allah (п·ә) in regard to your wife. He has allowed the wife of Hilal bin Umaiyyah to serve him.' I said, 'I would not seek permission from Messenger of Allah (п·ә) for I do not know what Messenger of Allah might say in response to that, as I am a young man'. It was in this state that I spent ten more nights and thus fifty days had passed since people boycotted us and gave up talking to us. After I had offered my Fajr prayer on the early morning of the fiftieth day of this boycott on the roof of one of our houses, and had sat in the very state which Allah described as: 'The earth seemed constrained for me despite its vastness', I heard the voice of a proclaimer from the peak of the hill Sal' shouting at the top of his voice: 'O Ka'b bin Malik, rejoice.' I fell down in prostration and came to know that there was (a message of) relief for me. Messenger of Allah (п·ә) had informed the people about the acceptance of our repentance by Allah after he had offered the Fajr prayer. So the people went on to give us glad tidings and some of them went to my companions in order to give them the glad tidings. A man spurred his horse towards me (to give the good news), and another one from the tribe of Aslam came running for the same purpose and, as he approached the mount, I received the good news which reached me before the rider did. When the one whose voice I had heard came to me to congratulate me, I took off my garments and gave them to him for the good news he brought to me. By Allah, I possessed nothing else (in the form of clothes) except these garments, at that time. Then I borrowed two garments, dressed myself and came to Messenger of Allah (п·ә) On my way, I met groups of people who greeted me for (the acceptance of) repentance and they said: 'Congratulations for acceptance of your repentance.' I reached the mosque where Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was sitting amidst people. Talhah bin 'Ubaidullah got up and rushed towards me, shook hands with me and greeted me. By Allah, no person stood up (to greet me) from amongst the Muhajirun besides him." Ka'b said that he never forgot (this good gesture of) Talhah. Ka'b further said: "I greeted Messenger of Allah (п·ә) with 'As-salamu 'alaikum' and his face was beaming with pleasure. He (п·ә) said, 'Rejoice with the best day you have ever seen since your mother gave you birth. 'I said: 'O Messenger of Allah! Is this (good news) from you or from Allah?' He said, 'No, it is from Allah.' And it was common with Messenger of Allah (п·ә) that when ever he was happy, his face would glow as if it were a part of the moon and it was from this that we recognized it (his delight). As I sat before him, I said, I have placed a condition upon myself that if Allah accepts my Taubah, I would give up all of my property in charity for the sake of Allah and His Messenger (п·ә)!' Thereupon Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, 'Keep some property with you, as it is better for you.' I said, 'I shall keep with me that portion which is in Khaibar'. I added: 'O Messenger of Allah! Verily, Allah has granted me salvation because of my truthfulness, and therefore, repentance obliges me to speak nothing but the truth as long as I am alive." Ka'b added: "By Allah, I do not know anyone among the Muslims who has been granted truthfulness better than me since I said this to the Prophet (п·ә). By Allah! Since the time I made a pledge of this to Messenger of Allah (п·ә), I have never intended to tell a lie, and I hope that Allah would protect me (against telling lies) for the rest of my life. Allah, the Exalted, the Glorious, revealed these Verses: 'Allah has forgiven the Prophet (п·ә), the Muhajirun (Muslim Emigrants who left their homes and came to Al-Madinah) and the Ansar (Muslims of Al- Madinah) who followed him (Muhammad (п·ә)) in the time of distress (Tabuk expedition), after the hearts of a party of them had nearly deviated (from the Right Path), but He accepted their repentance. Certainly, He is unto them full of kindness, Most Merciful. And (He did forgive also) the three who did not join [the Tabuk expedition and whose case was deferred (by the Prophet (п·ә)) for Allah's Decision] till for them the earth, vast as it is, was straitened and their ownselves were straitened to them, and they perceived that there is no fleeing from Allah, and no refuge but with Him. Then, He forgave them (accepted their repentance), that they might beg for His Pardon [repent (unto Him)]. Verily, Allah is the One Who forgives and accepts repentance, Most Merciful. O you who believe! Be afraid of Allah, and be with those who are true (in word and deeds)." (9:117,118). Ka'b said: "By Allah, since Allah guided me to Islam, there has been no blessing more significant for me than this truth of mine which I spoke to Messenger of Allah (п·ә), and if I were to tell a lie I would have been ruined as were ruined those who had told lies, for Allah described those who told lies with the worst description He ever attributed to anybody else, as He sent down the Revelation: They will swear by Allah to you (Muslims) when you return to them, that you may turn away from them. So turn away from them. Surely, they are Rijsun [i.e., Najasun (impure) because of their evil deeds], and Hell is their dwelling place - a recompense for that which they used to earn. They (the hypocrites) swear to you (Muslims) that you may be pleased with them, but if you are pleased with them, certainly Allah is not pleased with the people who are Al- Fa'siqun (rebellious, disobedient to Allah)". (9:95,96) Ka'b further added: "The matter of the three of us remained pending for decision apart from the case of those who had made excuses on oath before Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and he accepted those, took fresh oaths of allegiance from them and supplicated for their forgiveness. The Prophet (п·ә) kept our matter pending till Allah decided it. The three whose matter was deferred have been shown mercy. The reference here is not to our staying back from the expedition but to his delaying our matter and keeping it pending beyond the matter of those who made their excuses on oath which he accepted". [Al- Bukhari and Muslim] Another version adds: "Messenger of Allah (п·ә) set out for Tabuk on Thursday. He used to prefer to set out on journey on Thursday." Another version says: "Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to come back from a journey in the early forenoon and went straight to the mosque where he would perform two Rak'ah prayer. Afterwards he would seat himself there".
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ ЩғШ№ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩғШҢ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШ§ШҰШҜ ЩғШ№ШЁ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШЁЩҶЩҠЩҮ ШӯЩҠЩҶ Ш№Щ…ЩҠ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ЩғШ№ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩҠШӯШҜШ« ШЁШӯШҜЩҠШ«ЩҮ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӘШЁЩҲЩғвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩғШ№ШЁвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Щ… Ш§ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҢ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШәШІШ§ЩҮШ§ ЩӮШ· ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӘШЁЩҲЩғШҢ ШәЩҠШұ ШЈЩҶЩҠ ЩӮШҜ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒШӘ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШЁШҜШұШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ№Ш§ШӘШЁ ШЈШӯШҜ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ Ш®ШұШ¬ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҲЩҶ ЩҠШұЩҠШҜЩҲЩҶ Ш№ЩҠШұ ЩӮШұЩҠШҙ ШӯШӘЩү Ш¬Щ…Ш№ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮЩ… ЩҲШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш№ШҜЩҲЩҮЩ… Ш№Щ„Щү ШәЩҠШұ Щ…ЩҠШ№Ш§ШҜвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩ„ЩӮШҜ ШҙЩҮШҜШӘ Щ…Ш№ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш§Щ„Ш№ЩӮШЁШ© ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШӘЩҲШ§Ш«ЩӮЩҶШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ШҘШіЩ„Ш§Щ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ШЈШӯШЁ ШЈЩҶ Щ„ЩҠ ШЁЩҮШ§ Щ…ШҙЩҮШҜ ШЁШҜШұЩҚШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ШЁШҜШұ ШЈШ°ЩғШұ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші Щ…ЩҶЩҮШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ Ш®ШЁШұЩҠ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…ШҢ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӘШЁЩҲЩғ ШЈЩҶЩҠ Щ„Щ… ШЈЩғЩҶ ЩӮШ· ШЈЩӮЩҲЩү ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШЈЩҠШіШұ Щ…ЩҶЩҠ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒШӘ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ШӘЩ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШәШІЩҲШ©ШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ Ш¬Щ…Ш№ШӘ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮШ§ ШұШ§ШӯЩ„ШӘЩҠЩҶ ЩӮШ· ШӯШӘЩү Ш¬Щ…Ш№ШӘЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ ШӘЩ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШәШІЩҲШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩғЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШұЩҠШҜ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩҲШұЩү ШЁШәЩҠШұЩҮШ§ ШӯШӘЩү ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ШӘЩ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШәШІЩҲШ©ШҢ ЩҒШәШІШ§ЩҮШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠ ШӯШұ ШҙШҜЩҠШҜШҢ ЩҲШ§ШіШӘЩӮШЁЩ„ ШіЩҒШұШ§ЩӢ ШЁШ№ЩҠШҜШ§ЩӢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҒШ§ШІШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШ§ШіШӘЩӮШЁЩ„ Ш№ШҜШҜШ§ЩӢ ЩғШ«ЩҠШұШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒШ¬Щ„Щү Щ„Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҮЩ… Щ„ЩҠШӘШЈЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШЈЩҮШЁШ© ШәШІЩҲЩҮЩ… ЩҒШЈШ®ШЁШұЩҮЩ… ШЁЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ ЩҠШұЩҠШҜШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҲЩҶ Щ…Ш№ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩғШ«ЩҠШұ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШ¬Щ…Ш№ЩҮЩ… ЩғШӘШ§ШЁ ШӯШ§ЩҒШё вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҠШұЩҠШҜ ШЁШ°Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҠЩҲШ§ЩҶвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩғШ№ШЁвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҠШұЩҠШҜ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШӘШәЩҠШЁ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШёЩҶ ШЈЩҶ Ш°Щ„Щғ ШіЩҠШ®ЩҒЩү ШЁЩҮ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щ… ЩҠЩҶШІЩ„ ЩҒЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШӯЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШәШІШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШӘЩ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӯЩҠЩҶ Ш·Ш§ШЁШӘ Ш§Щ„Ш«Щ…Ш§Шұ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШёЩ„Ш§Щ„ ЩҒШЈЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ ШЈШөШ№Шұ ЩҒШӘШ¬ЩҮШІ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҲЩҶ Щ…Ш№ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШ·ЩҒЩӮШӘ ШЈШәШҜЩҲ Щ„ЩғЩҠ ШЈШӘШ¬ЩҮШІ Щ…Ш№ЩҮШҢ ЩҒШЈШұШ¬Ш№ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ШЈЩӮШ¶ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШЈЩӮЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩҶШ§ ЩӮШ§ШҜШұ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш°Щ„Щғ ШҘШ°Ш§ ШЈШұШҜШӘШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШІЩ„ ЩҠШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜЩү ШЁЩҠ ШӯШӘЩү Ш§ШіШӘЩ…Шұ ШЁШ§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШҜШҢ ЩҒШЈШөШЁШӯ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШәШ§ШҜЩҠШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҲЩҶ Щ…Ш№ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ШЈЩӮШ¶ Щ…ЩҶ Ш¬ЩҮШ§ШІЩҠ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢШҢ Ш«Щ… ШәШҜЩҲШӘ ЩҒШұШ¬Ш№ШӘ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ШЈЩӮШ¶ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШІЩ„ ЩҠШӘЩ…Ш§ШҜЩү ШЁЩҠ ШӯШӘЩү ШЈШіШұШ№ЩҲШ§ ЩҲШӘЩҒШ§ШұШ· Ш§Щ„ШәШұЩҲШҢ ЩҒЩҮЩ…Щ…ШӘ ШЈЩҶ ШЈШұШӘШӯЩ„ ЩҒШЈШҜШұЩғЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШ§Щ„ЩҠШӘЩҶЩҠ ЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘШҢ Ш«Щ… Щ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮШҜШұ Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҒШ·ЩҒЩӮШӘ ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш®ШұШ¬ШӘ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш®ШұЩҲШ¬ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШӯШІЩҶЩҶЩҠ ШЈЩҶЩҠ ШЈШұЩү Щ„ЩҠ ШЈШіЩҲШ©ШҢ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШұШ¬Щ„Ш§ЩӢ Щ…ШәЩ…ЩҲШөШ§ЩӢ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҒШ§ЩӮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ"вҖҸ ШЈЩ…Ш§ ЩҮШ°Ш§ ЩҒЩӮШҜ ШөШҜЩӮШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ… ШӯШӘЩү ЩҠЩӮШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠЩғвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲШіШ§Шұ ШұШ¬Ш§Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ ШЁЩҶЩҠ ШіЩ„Щ…Ш© ЩҒШ§ШӘШЁШ№ЩҲЩҶЩҠШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ Ш№Щ„Щ…ЩҶШ§Щғ ШЈШ°ЩҶШЁШӘ Ш°ЩҶШЁШ§ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ЩҮШ°Ш§ШҢ Щ„ЩӮШҜ Ш№Ш¬ШІШӘ ЩҒЩҠ ШЈЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩғЩҲЩҶ Ш§Ш№ШӘШ°ШұШӘ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЁЩ…Ш§ Ш§Ш№ШӘШ°Шұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҲЩҶ ЩҒЩӮШҜ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҒЩҠЩғ Ш°ЩҶШЁЩғ Ш§ШіШӘШәЩҒШ§Шұ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Щ„ЩғвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШІШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§ ЩҠШӨЩҶШЁЩҲЩҶЩҶЩҠ ШӯШӘЩү ШЈШұШҜШӘ ШЈЩҶ ШЈШұШ¬Ш№ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒШЈЩғШ°ШЁ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩӮЩ„ШӘ Щ„ЩҮЩ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩ„ Щ„ЩӮЩҠ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Щ…Ш№ЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШӯШҜвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ… Щ„ЩӮЩҠЩҮ Щ…Ш№Щғ ШұШ¬Щ„Ш§ЩҶ ЩӮШ§Щ„Ш§ Щ…Ш«Щ„ Щ…Ш§ ЩӮЩ„ШӘШҢ ЩҲЩӮЩҠЩ„ Щ„ЩҮЩ…Ш§ Щ…Ш«Щ„ Щ…Ш§ ЩӮЩҠЩ„ Щ„ЩғШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҮЩ…Ш§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…ШұШ§ШұШ© ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШұШЁЩҠШ№ Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ…ШұЩҠШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШЁЩҶ ШЈЩ…ЩҠШ© Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ЩӮЩҒЩҠвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ°ЩғШұЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҠ ШұШ¬Щ„ЩҠЩҶ ШөШ§Щ„ШӯЩҠЩҶ ЩӮШҜ ШҙЩҮШҜШ§ ШЁШҜШұШ§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҠЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈШіЩҲШ©вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩ…Ш¶ЩҠШӘ ШӯЩҠЩҶ Ш°ЩғШұЩҲЩҮЩ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҠвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩҶЩҮЩү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш№ЩҶ ЩғЩ„Ш§Щ…ЩҶШ§ ШЈЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© Щ…ЩҶ ШЁЩҠЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ§Ш¬ШӘЩҶШЁЩҶШ§ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші- ШЈЩҲ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӘШәЩҠШұЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҶШ§- ШӯШӘЩү ШӘЩҶЩғШұШӘ Щ„ЩҠ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШі Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ШҢ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ ЩҮЩҠ ШЁШ§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҠ ШЈШ№ШұЩҒШҢ ЩҒЩ„ШЁШ«ЩҶШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш®Щ…ШіЩҠЩҶ Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш©вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒШЈЩ…Ш§ ШөШ§ШӯШЁШ§ЩҠ ЩҒШ§ШіШӘЩғШ§ЩҶШ§ ЩҲЩӮШ№ШҜШ§ ЩҒЩҠ ШЁЩҠЩҲШӘЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҠШЁЩғЩҠШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲШЈЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶШ§ ЩҒЩғЩҶШӘ ШЈШҙШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҲЩ… ЩҲШЈШ¬Щ„ШҜЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҒЩғЩҶШӘ ШЈШ®ШұШ¬ ЩҒШЈШҙЩҮШҜ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© Щ…Ш№ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶШҢ ЩҲШЈШ·ЩҲЩҒ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШЈШіЩҲШ§ЩӮ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠЩғЩ„Щ…ЩҶЩҠ ШЈШӯШҜШҢ ЩҲШўШӘЩҠ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒШЈШіЩ„Щ… Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ЩҒЩҠ Щ…Ш¬Щ„ШіЩҮ ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш©ШҢ ЩҒШЈЩӮЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩ„ ШӯШұЩғ ШҙЩҒШӘЩҠЩҮ ШЁШұШҜ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ШЈЩ… вҖҸШҹвҖҸ Ш«Щ… ШЈШөЩ„ЩҠ ЩӮШұЩҠШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШЈШіШ§ШұЩӮЩҮ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШёШұШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ШЈЩӮШЁЩ„ШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү ШөЩ„Ш§ШӘЩҠ ЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҲШҘШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҒШӘ ЩҶШӯЩҲЩҮ ШЈШ№ШұШ¶ Ш№ЩҶЩҠШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш·Ш§Щ„ Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ Ш¬ЩҒЩҲШ© Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶ Щ…ШҙЩҠШӘ ШӯШӘЩү ШӘШіЩҲШұШӘ Ш¬ШҜШ§Шұ ШӯШ§ШҰШ· ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩӮШӘШ§ШҜШ© ЩҲЩҮЩҲ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…ЩҠ ЩҲШЈШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШҘЩ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҒШіЩ„Щ…ШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҒЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШұШҜЩ‘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…ШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШЈШЁШ§ ЩӮШӘШ§ШҜШ© ШЈЩҶШҙШҜЩғ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҮЩ„ ШӘШ№Щ„Щ…ЩҶЩҠ ШЈЩҸШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШұШіЩҲЩ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒШіЩғШӘШҢ ЩҒШ№ШҜШӘ ЩҒЩҶШ§ШҙШҜШӘЩҮ ЩҒШіЩғШӘШҢ ЩҒШ№ШҜШӘ ЩҒЩҶШ§ШҙШҜШӘЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШұШіЩҲЩ„ЩҮ ШЈШ№Щ„Щ…вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩҒШ§Ш¶ШӘ Ш№ЩҠЩҶШ§ЩҠШҢ ЩҲШӘЩҲЩ„ЩҠШӘ ШӯШӘЩү ШӘШіЩҲШұШӘ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШҜШ§ШұШҢ ЩҒШЁЩҠЩҶЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶШ§ ШЈЩ…ШҙЩү ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩҲЩӮ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҜЩҠЩҶШ© ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҶШЁШ·Щү Щ…ЩҶ ЩҶШЁШ· ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШҙШ§Щ… Щ…Щ…ЩҶ ЩӮШҜЩ… ШЁШ§Щ„Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ… ШЁШЁЩҠШ№ЩҮ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ…ШҜЩҠЩҶШ© ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҠШҜЩ„ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩғШ№ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒШ·ЩҒЩӮ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҠШҙЩҠШұЩҲЩҶ Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩ„ЩҠ ШӯШӘЩү Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҶЩү ЩҒШҜЩҒШ№ ШҘЩ„ЩҠ ЩғШӘШ§ШЁ Щ…ЩҶ Щ…Щ„Щғ ШәШіШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩғЩҶШӘ ЩғШ§ШӘШЁШ§ЩӢвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШұШЈШӘЩҮ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ…Ш§ ШЁШ№ШҜ ЩҒШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШҜ ШЁЩ„ШәЩҶШ§ ШЈЩҶ ШөШ§ШӯШЁЩғ ЩӮШҜ Ш¬ЩҒШ§ЩғШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ¬Ш№Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁШҜШ§Шұ ЩҮЩҲШ§ЩҶ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ Щ…Ш¶ЩҠШ№Ш©ШҢ ЩҒШ§Щ„ШӯЩӮ ШЁЩҶШ§ ЩҶЩҲШ§ШіЩғШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ЩӮШұШЈШӘЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲЩҮШ°ЩҮ ШЈЩҠШ¶Ш§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩ„Ш§ШЎ ЩҒШӘЩҠЩ…Щ…ШӘ ШЁЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҶЩҲШұ ЩҒШіШ¬ШұШӘЩҮШ§ШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ Щ…Ш¶ШӘ ШЈШұШЁШ№ЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ…ШіЩҠЩҶ ЩҲШ§ШіШӘЩ„ШЁШ« Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШЈШӘЩҠЩҶЩүШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШЈЩ…ШұЩғ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ№ШӘШІЩ„ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩғШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШ·Щ„ЩӮЩҮШ§ШҢ ШЈЩ… Щ…Ш§Ш°Ш§ ШЈЩҒШ№Щ„вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ШҢ ШЁЩ„ Ш§Ш№ШӘШІЩ„ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩӮШұШЁЩҶЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲШЈШұШіЩ„ ШҘЩ„Щү ШөШ§ШӯШЁЩҠ ШЁЩ…Ш«Щ„ Ш°Щ„ЩғвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘ Щ„Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ„ШӯЩӮЩҠ ШЁШЈЩҮЩ„Щғ ЩҒЩғЩҲЩҶЩҠ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮЩ… ШӯШӘЩү ЩҠЩӮШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ…ШұШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш§ШЎШӘ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШ© ЩҮЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШЁЩҶ ШЈЩ…ЩҠШ© ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Щ„ЩҮ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩҶ ЩҮЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШЁЩҶ ШЈЩ…ЩҠШ© ШҙЩҠШ® Ш¶Ш§ШҰШ№ Щ„ЩҠШі Щ„ЩҮ Ш®Ш§ШҜЩ…ШҢ ЩҒЩҮЩ„ ШӘЩғШұЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШЈШ®ШҜЩ…ЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩӮШұШЁЩҶЩғвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШӯШұЩғШ© ШҘЩ„Щү ШҙЩҠШЎШҢ ЩҲЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШІШ§Щ„ ЩҠШЁЩғЩҠ Щ…ЩҶШ° ЩғШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШҘЩ„Щү ЩҠЩҲЩ…ЩҮ ЩҮШ°Ш§вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ЩҠ ШЁШ№Ш¶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ Ш§ШіШӘШЈШ°ЩҶШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩғШҢ ЩҒЩӮШҜ ШЈШ°ЩҶ Щ„Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШ© ЩҮЩ„Ш§Щ„ ШЁЩҶ ШЈЩ…ЩҠШ© ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ®ШҜЩ…ЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ ШЈШіШӘШЈШ°ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ЩҠШҜШұЩҠЩҶЩҠ Щ…Ш§Ш°Ш§ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…ШҢ ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш§ШіШӘШЈШ°ЩҶШӘЩҮ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ ЩҲШЈЩҶШ§ ШұШ¬Щ„ ШҙШ§ШЁвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩ„ШЁШ«ШӘ ШЁШ°Щ„Щғ Ш№ШҙШұ Щ„ЩҠШ§Щ„ЩҚШҢ ЩҒЩғЩ…Щ„ Щ„ЩҶШ§ Ш®Щ…ШіЩҲЩҶ Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш© Щ…ЩҶ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ЩҶЩҮЩү Ш№ЩҶ ЩғЩ„Ш§Щ…ЩҶШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ Ш«Щ… ШөЩ„ЩҠШӘ ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ¬Шұ ШөШЁШ§Шӯ Ш®Щ…ШіЩҠЩҶ Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш№Щ„Щү ШёЩҮШұ ШЁЩҠШӘ Щ…ЩҶ ШЁЩҠЩҲШӘЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩҒШЁЩҠЩҶЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶШ§ Ш¬Ш§Щ„Ші Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ШӯШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩү Ш°ЩғШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү Щ…ЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩӮШҜ Ш¶Ш§ЩҒШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҲШ¶Ш§ЩӮШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ШЁЩ…Ш§ ШұШӯШЁШӘШҢ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШөЩҲШӘ ШөШ§ШұШ® ШЈЩҲЩҒЩү Ш№Щ„Щү ШіЩ„Ш№ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ ШЁШЈШ№Щ„Щү ШөЩҲШӘЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ЩғШ№ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ ШЈШЁШҙШұ ЩҒШ®ШұШұШӘ ШіШ§Ш¬ШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШ№ШұЩҒШӘ ШЈЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШҜ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ЩҒШұШ¬вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒШўШ°ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШЁШӘЩҲШЁШ© Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҶШ§ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШөЩ„Щү ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ¬Шұ ЩҒШ°ЩҮШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҠШЁШҙШұЩҲЩҶЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩҒШ°ЩҮШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ШөШ§ШӯШЁЩҠ Щ…ШЁШҙШұЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲШұЩғШ¶ ШұШ¬Щ„ ШҘЩ„ЩҠ ЩҒШұШіШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШіШ№Щү ШіШ§Ш№ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҠ ЩҲШЈЩҲЩҒЩү Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШЁЩ„ШҢ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШөЩҲШӘ ШЈШіШұШ№ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҒШұШіШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ Ш¬Ш§ШЎЩҶЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш°Щү ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШөЩҲШӘЩҮ ЩҠШЁШҙШұЩҶЩҠ ЩҶШІШ№ШӘ Щ„ЩҮ Ш«ЩҲШЁЩҠ ЩҒЩғШіЩҲШӘЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШҘЩҠШ§ЩҮ ШЁШЁШҙШұШ§ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩ…Щ„Щғ ШәЩҠШұЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩҲЩ…ШҰШ°ШҢ ЩҲШ§ШіШӘШ№ШұШӘ Ш«ЩҲШЁЩҠЩҶ ЩҒЩ„ШЁШіШӘЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҲШ§ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮШӘ ШЈШӘШЈЩ…Щ… ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШӘЩ„ЩӮШ§ЩҶЩү Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҒЩҲШ¬Ш§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҲШ¬Ш§ЩӢ ЩҠЩҮЩҶШҰЩҲЩҶЩҠ ШЁШ§Щ„ШӘЩҲШЁШ© ЩҲЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶ Щ„ЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ШӘЩҮЩҶЩғ ШӘЩҲШЁШ© Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҜШ®Щ„ШӘ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш¬Ш§Щ„Ші ШӯЩҲЩ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШіШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ… Ш·Щ„ШӯШ© ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩҠЩҮШұЩҲЩ„ ШӯШӘЩү ШөШ§ЩҒШӯЩҶЩҠ ЩҲЩҮЩҶШЈЩҶЩҠШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ… ШұШ¬Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬ШұЩҠЩҶ ШәЩҠШұЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩғШ№ШЁ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩҶШіШ§ЩҮШ§ Щ„Ш·Щ„ШӯШ©вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩғШ№ШЁвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ШіЩ„Щ…ШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ЩҠШЁШұЩӮ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШіШұЩҲШұ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШЁШҙШұ ШЁШ®ЩҠШұ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Щ…ШұЩ‘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғ Щ…Ш° ЩҲЩ„ШҜШӘЩғ ШЈЩ…ЩғШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ…ЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩғ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ ШҢ ШЁЩ„ Щ…ЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ШҢ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҘШ°Ш§ ШіШұ Ш§ШіШӘЩҶШ§Шұ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩҮ ШӯШӘЩү ЩғШЈЩҶ ЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩҮ ЩӮШ·Ш№Ш© ЩӮЩ…ШұШҢ ЩҲЩғЩҶШ§ ЩҶШ№ШұЩҒ Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ…ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ Ш¬Щ„ШіШӘ ШЁЩҠЩҶ ЩҠШҜЩҠЩҮ ЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘЩҲШЁШӘЩҠ ШЈЩҶ ШЈЩҶШ®Щ„Ш№ Щ…ЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩҠ ШөШҜЩӮШ©вҖҺ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШҘЩ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ…ШіЩғ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғ ШЁШ№Ш¶ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ ЩҒЩҮЩҲ Ш®ЩҠШұ Щ„ЩғШҢ ЩҒЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩҠ ШЈЩ…ШіЩғ ШіЩҮЩ…ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш°Щү ШЁШ®ЩҠШЁШұвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩӮЩ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶШ¬Ш§ЩҶЩҠ ШЁШ§Щ„ШөШҜЩӮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘЩҲШЁШӘЩҠ ШЈЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ШЈШӯШҜШ«ЩҺ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШөШҜЩӮШ§ЩӢ Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩӮЩҠШӘ ШҢ ЩҒЩҲ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ Ш№Щ„Щ…ШӘ ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶ ШЈШЁЩ„Ш§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ШөШҜЩӮ Ш§Щ„ШӯШҜЩҠШ« Щ…ЩҶШ° Ш°ЩғШұШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ„ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЈШӯШіЩҶ Щ…Щ…Ш§ ШЈШЁЩ„Ш§ЩҶЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШӘШ№Щ…ШҜШӘ ЩғШ°ШЁШ© Щ…ЩҶШ° ЩӮЩ„ШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ„ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҘЩ„Щү ЩҠЩҲЩ…ЩҠ ЩҮШ°Ш§ШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩҠ Щ„ШЈШұШ¬ЩҲ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШӯЩҒШёЩҶЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҒЩҠЩ…Ш§ ШЁЩӮЩҠШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШЈЩҶШІЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸЩ„ЩӮШҜ ШӘШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…ЩҮШ§Ш¬ШұЩҠЩҶ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШЈЩҶШөШ§Шұ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ Ш§ШӘШЁШ№ЩҲЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ШіШ§Ш№Ш© Ш§Щ„Ш№ШіШұШ©вҖҸ)вҖҸ ШӯШӘЩү ШЁЩ„ШәвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸШҘЩҶЩҮ ШЁЩҮЩ… ШұШӨЩҲЩҒ ШұШӯЩҠЩ… вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҲШ§ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш¶Ш§ЩӮШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ШЁЩ…Ш§ ШұШӯШЁШӘвҖҸ}вҖҸ ШӯШӘЩү ШЁЩ„Шә вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸШ§ШӘЩӮЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩғЩҲЩҶЩҲШ§ Щ…Ш№ Ш§Щ„ШөШ§ШҜЩӮЩҠЩҶвҖҸ}вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШ§Щ„ШӘЩҲШЁШ© 117ШҢ 119вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩғШ№ШЁ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶШ№Щ… Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҶШ№Щ…Ш© ЩӮШ· ШЁШ№ШҜ ШҘШ° ЩҮШҜШ§ЩҶЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„Щ„ШҘШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ШЈШ№ШёЩ… ЩҒЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ ШөШҜЩӮЩҠ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЈЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ШЈЩғЩҲЩҶ ЩғШ°ШЁШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҒШЈЩҮЩ„Щғ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ЩҮЩ„Щғ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ЩғШ°ШЁЩҲШ§ШҢ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ЩғШ°ШЁЩҲШ§ ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШЈЩҶШІЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯЩҠ ШҙШұ Щ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ШЈШӯШҜШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸШіЩҠШӯЩ„ЩҒЩҲЩҶ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„ЩғЩ… ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш§ЩҶЩӮЩ„ШЁШӘЩ… ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… Щ„ШӘШ№ШұШ¶ЩҲШ§ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ… ЩҒШЈШ№ШұШ¶ЩҲШ§ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ… ШҘЩҶЩҮЩ… ШұШ¬Ші ЩҲЩ…ШЈЩҲШ§ЩҮЩ… Ш¬ЩҮЩҶЩ… Ш¬ШІШ§ШЎ ШЁЩ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҲШ§ ЩҠЩғШіШЁЩҲЩҶ ЩҠШӯЩ„ЩҒЩҲЩҶ Щ„ЩғЩ… Щ„ШӘШұШ¶ЩҲШ§ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ… ЩҒШҘЩҶ ШӘШұШ¶ЩҲШ§ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ… ЩҒШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШұШ¶Щү Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ§ШіЩӮЩҠЩҶвҖҸ}вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШ§Щ„ШӘЩҲШЁШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ 95ШҢ96вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩғШ№ШЁ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҶШ§ Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҶШ§ ШЈЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© Ш№ЩҶ ШЈЩ…Шұ ШЈЩҲЩ„ШҰЩғ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ЩӮШЁЩ„ Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ… ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШӯЩ„ЩҒЩҲШ§ Щ„ЩҮ ШҢ ЩҒШЁШ§ЩҠШ№ЩҮЩ… ЩҲШ§ШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Щ„ЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲШЈШұШ¬ШЈ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЈЩ…ШұЩҶШ§ ШӯШӘЩү ЩӮШ¶Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҒЩҠЩҮ ШЁШ°Щ„ЩғШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸЩҲШ№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҲШ§вҖҸ}вҖҸ ЩҲЩ„ЩҠШі Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ Ш°ЩғШұ Щ…Щ…Ш§ Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҶШ§ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҒЩҶШ§ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШәШІЩҲШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ ЩҮЩҲ ШӘШ®Щ„ЩҠЩҒЩҮ ШҘЩҠШ§ЩҶШ§ ЩҲШҘШұШ¬Ш§ШӨЩҮ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҶШ§ Ш№Щ…ЩҶ ШӯЩ„ЩҒ Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ§Ш№ШӘШ°Шұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШЁЩ„ Щ…ЩҶЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ Щ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© вҖҸ"вҖҸШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш®ШұШ¬ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӘШЁЩҲЩғ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ…ЩҠШіШҢ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҠШӯШЁ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШ®ШұШ¬ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ…ЩҠШівҖҸ"вҖҸ
ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩӮШҜЩ… Щ…ЩҶ ШіЩҒШұ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩҶЩҮШ§ШұШ§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¶ШӯЩүШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩӮШҜЩ… ШЁШҜШЈ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜ ЩҒШөЩ„Щү ЩҒЩҠЩҮ ШұЩғШ№ШӘЩҠЩҶ Ш«Щ… Ш¬Щ„Ші ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Imran bin Al-Husain Al-Khuza'i (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: A woman from the tribe Juhainah came to Messenger of Allah (п·ә) while she was pregnant from (Zina) adultery and said to him: "O Messenger of Allah! I have committed an offense liable to Hadd (prescribed punishment), so exact the execution of the sentence." Messenger of Allah (п·ә) called her guardian and said to him, "Treat her kindly. Bring her to me after the delivery of the child." That man complied with the orders. At last the Prophet (п·ә) commanded to carry out the sentence. Her clothes were secured around her and she was stoned to death. The Prophet (п·ә) led her funeral prayers. 'Umar submitted: "O Messenger of Allah! She committed Zina and you have performed funeral prayer for her?" He replied, "Verily, she made repentance which would suffice for seventy of the people of Al-Madinah if it is divided among them. Can there be any higher degree of repentance than that she sacrificed her life voluntarily to win the Pleasure of Allah, the Exalted?". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҶШ¬ЩҠШҜ- Ш¶Щ… Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҲЩҶ ЩҲЩҒШӘШӯ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҠЩ… - Ш№Щ…ШұШ§ЩҶ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӯШөЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШІШ§Ш№Щү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШ© Щ…ЩҶ Ш¬ЩҮЩҠЩҶШ© ШЈШӘШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҲЩҮЩү ШӯШЁЩ„Щү Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШІЩҶЩүШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈШөШЁШӘ ШӯШҜШ§ЩӢ ЩҒШЈЩӮЩ…ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҒШҜШ№Ш§ ЩҶШЁЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҲЩ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШӯШіЩҶ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҲШ¶Ш№ШӘ ЩҒШЈШӘЩҶЩҠШҢ ЩҒЩҒШ№Щ„ ЩҒШЈЩ…Шұ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҶШЁЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…ШҢ ЩҒШҙШҜШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш«ЩҠШ§ШЁЩҮШ§ШҢ Ш«Щ… ШЈЩ…Шұ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҒШұШ¬Щ…ШӘШҢ Ш«Щ… ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШўЩ„ЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ…ШұвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӘШөЩ„Щү Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШІЩҶШӘШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩӮШҜ ШӘШ§ШЁШӘ ШӘЩҲШЁШ© Щ„ЩҲ ЩӮЩ…ШіШӘ ШЁЩҠЩҶ ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҜЩҠЩҶШ© Щ„ЩҲШіШӘШ№ШӘЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩ„ ЩҲШ¬ШҜШӘ ШЈЩҒШ¶Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҶ Ш¬Ш§ШҜШӘ ШЁЩҶЩҒШіЩҮШ§ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ вҖҸШҹвҖҸвҖҸ!вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Abbas and Anas bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "If a son of Adam were to own a valley full of gold, he would desire to have two. Nothing can fill his mouth except the earth (of the grave). Allah turns with mercy to him who turns to Him in repentance". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ ШЈЩҶ Щ„Ш§ШЁЩҶ ШўШҜЩ… ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш°ЩҮШЁ ШЈШӯШЁ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩғЩҲЩҶ Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҶ ЩҠЩ…Щ„ШЈ ЩҒШ§ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШӘШұШ§ШЁШҢ ЩҲЩҠШӘЩҲШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„Щү Щ…ЩҶ ШӘШ§ШЁвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Allah, the Exalted, smiles at two men, one of them killed the other and both will enter Jannah. The first is killed by the other while he is fighting in the Cause of Allah, and thereafter Allah will turn in mercy to the second and guide him to accept Islam and then he dies as a Shaheed (martyr) fighting in the Cause of Allah." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩҠШ¶ШӯЩғ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШіШЁШӯШ§ЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШ¬Щ„ЩҠЩҶ ЩҠЩӮШӘЩ„ ШЈШӯШҜЩҮЩ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШўШ®Шұ ЩҠШҜШ®Щ„Ш§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҠЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ЩҮШ°Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ ШіШЁЩҠЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠЩӮШӘЩ„ШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩҠШӘЩҲШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ЩӮШ§ШӘЩ„ ЩҒЩҠШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠШіШӘШҙЩҮШҜвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Malik Al-Harith bin Asim Al-Ash'ari (May Allah be pleased with him) reported that:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: "Purity is half of faith, and the praise of Allah fills the scale. Glorification and praise fill up what is between the heavens and the earth. Prayer is a light, charity is proof, and patience is illumination. The Quran is a proof for you or against you. All people go out early in the morning and sell themselves, either setting themselves free or ruining themselves.". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШӯШ§ШұШ« ШЁЩҶ Ш№Ш§ШөЩ… Ш§Щ„ШЈШҙШ№ШұЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШ§Щ„Ш·ЩҮЩҲШұ ШҙШ·Шұ Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҠЩ…Ш§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘЩ…Щ„ШЈ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҠШІШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲШіШЁШӯШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘЩ…Щ„ШўЩҶ -ШЈЩҲ ШӘЩ…Щ„ШЈ- Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ЩҲШ§ШӘ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© ЩҶЩҲШұШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШөШҜЩӮШ© ШЁШұЩҮШ§ЩҶШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШөШЁШұ Ш¶ЩҠШ§ШЎШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩӮШұШўЩҶ ШӯШ¬Ш© Щ„Щғ ШЈЩҲ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩғЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҠШәШҜЩҲШҢ ЩҒШЁШ§ШҰШ№ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҮ ЩҒЩ…Ш№ШӘЩӮЩҮШ§ШҢ ШЈЩҲ Щ…ЩҲШЁЩӮЩҮШ§вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) reported that:
hadiths.body: Certain people of the Ansar asked the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and he gave them; then they again asked him and he gave them until all what he possessed was exhausted. Then the Prophet (п·ә) said, "Whatever wealth I have, I will not withhold from you. Whosoever would be chaste and modest; Allah will keep him chaste and modest and whosoever would seek self-sufficiency, Allah will make him self-sufficient; and whosoever would be patient, Allah will give him patience, and no one is granted a gift better and more comprehensive than patience". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ ШіШ№ШҜ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щғ ШЁЩҶ ШіЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШҜШұЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШЈЩҶ ЩҶШ§ШіШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШөШ§Шұ ШіШЈЩ„ЩҲШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒШЈШ№Ш·Ш§ЩҮЩ…ШҢ Ш«Щ… ШіШЈЩ„ЩҲЩҮ ЩҒШЈШ№Ш·Ш§ЩҮЩ… ШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ЩҶЩҒШҜ Щ…Ш§ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШЈЩҶЩҒЩӮ ЩғЩ„ ШҙЩҠШЎ ШЁЩҠШҜЩҮ вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩғЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ Ш®ЩҠШұ ЩҒЩ„ЩҶ ШЈШҜШ®ШұЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩғЩ… ШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩҠШіШӘШ№ЩҒЩҒ ЩҠШ№ЩҒЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩҠШіШӘШәЩҶ ЩҠШәЩҶЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩҠШӘШөШЁШұ ЩҠШөШЁШұЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ШЈШ№Ш·ЩҠ ШЈШӯШҜ Ш№Ш·Ш§ШЎЩӢ Ш®ЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШЈЩҲШіШ№ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШөШЁШұвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Yahya Suhaib bin Sinan (May Allah be pleased with him) reported that:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "How wonderful is the case of a believer; there is good for him in everything and this applies only to a believer. If prosperity attends him, he expresses gratitude to Allah and that is good for him; and if adversity befalls him, he endures it patiently and that is better for him". [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҠШӯЩҠЩү ШөЩҮЩҠШЁ ШЁЩҶ ШіЩҶШ§ЩҶ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ вҖҸШ№Ш¬ШЁШ§ Щ„ШЈЩ…Шұ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶ ШҘЩҶ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҮ ЩғЩ„ЩҮ Щ„ЩҮ Ш®ЩҠШұШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҠШі Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ„ШЈШӯШҜ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Щ„Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШЈШөШ§ШЁШӘЩҮ ШіШұШ§ШЎ ШҙЩғШұ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш®ЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ШЈШөШ§ШЁШӘЩҮ Ш¶ШұШ§ШЎ ШөШЁШұ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш®ЩҠШұШ§ЩӢ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: When the last illness of Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made him unconscious, Fatimah (May Allah be pleased with her) exclaimed: "Ah, the distress of my dear father." He (п·ә) said, "There will be no distress for your father after today". When he died she said: "My father, Allah has called you back and you have responded to His Call. O father! Garden of Firdaus is your abode. O father! We announce to Jibril your death." When he was buried, she said: "Are you satisfied now that you put earth over (the grave of) Messenger of Allah (п·ә)?" [Al- Bukhari]
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈЩҶШі ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Щ…Ш§ Ш«ЩӮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш¬Ш№Щ„ ЩҠШӘШәШҙШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ЩғШұШЁ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ ЩҒШ§Ш·Щ…Ш© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШ§ЩғШұШЁ ШЈШЁШӘШ§ЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩ„ЩҠШі Ш№Щ„Щү ШЈШЁЩҠЩғ ЩғШұШЁ ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ…вҖҸ вҖҸ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ Щ…Ш§ШӘ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШЈШЁШӘШ§ЩҮ ШЈШ¬Ш§ШЁ ШұШЁШ§ЩӢ ШҜШ№Ш§ЩҮШҢ ЩҠШ§ ШЈШЁШӘШ§ЩҮ Ш¬ЩҶШ© Ш§Щ„ЩҒШұШҜЩҲШі Щ…ШЈЩҲШ§ЩҮШҢ ЩҠШ§ ШЈШЁШӘШ§ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш¬ШЁШұЩҠЩ„ ЩҶЩҶШ№Ш§ЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ШҜЩҒЩҶ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ§Ш·Щ…Ш© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШ·Ш§ШЁШӘ ШЈЩҶЩҒШіЩғЩ… ШЈЩҶ ШӘШӯШ«ЩҲШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ШӘШұШ§ШЁвҖҸШҹвҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Usamah bin Zaid (May Allah be pleased with them) narrated:
hadiths.body: The daughter of the Prophet (п·ә) sent for him as her child was dying, but the Prophet (п·ә) returned the messenger and sent her good wishes saying, "Whatever Allah takes away or gives, belongs to Him, and everything with Him has a limited fixed term (in this world), and so she should be patient and anticipate Allah's reward." She again sent for him adjuring him for the sake of Allah to come. The Messenger of Allah, accompanied with Sa'd bin 'Ubadah, Mu'adh bin Jabal, Ubayy bin Ka'b, Zaid bin Thabit and some other men went to see her. The child was lifted up to the Messenger of Allah while his breath was disturbed in his chest. On seeing that, the eyes of the Prophet (п·ә) streamed with tears. Sa'd said, "O Messenger of Allah! What is this?" He replied, "It is compassion which Allah has placed in the hearts of His slaves, Allah is Compassionate only to those among His slaves who are compassionate (to others)". Another version says: Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Allah shows compassion only to those among His slaves who are compassionate". [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШІЩҠШҜ ШЈШіШ§Щ…Ш© ШЁЩҶ ШІЩҠШҜ ШЁЩҶ ШӯШ§ШұШ«Ш© Щ…ЩҲЩ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҲШӯШЁЩҮ ЩҲШ§ШЁЩҶ ШӯШЁЩҮШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШұШіЩ„ШӘ ШЁЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶЩҠ ЩӮШҜ Ш§ШӯШӘШ¶Шұ ЩҒШ§ШҙЩҮШҜЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩҒШЈШұШіЩ„ ЩҠЩӮШұШҰ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШ®Ш°ШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШ№Ш·ЩүШҢ ЩҲЩғЩ„ ШҙЩҠШЎ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮ ШЁШЈШ¬Щ„ Щ…ШіЩ…ЩүШҢ ЩҒЩ„ШӘШөШЁШұ ЩҲЩ„ШӘШӯШӘШіШЁвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҒШЈШұШіЩ„ШӘ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШӘЩӮШіЩ… Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ Щ„ЩҠШЈШӘЩҠЩҶЩҮШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ… ЩҲЩ…Ш№ЩҮ ШіШ№ШҜ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш№Ш§Ш° ШЁЩҶ Ш¬ШЁЩ„ШҢ ЩҲШЈШЁЩҠ ШЁЩҶ ЩғШ№ШЁШҢ ЩҲШІЩҠШҜ ШЁЩҶ Ш«Ш§ШЁШӘШҢ ЩҲШұШ¬Ш§Щ„ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҒШұЩҒШ№ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ШөШЁЩҠ ЩҒШЈЩӮШ№ШҜЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ШӯШ¬ШұЩҮ ЩҲЩҶЩҒШіЩҮ ШӘЩӮШ№ЩӮШ№ШҢ ЩҒЩҒШ§Ш¶ШӘ Ш№ЩҠЩҶШ§ЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШіШ№ШҜвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ЩҮШ°Ш§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҮШ°ЩҮ ШұШӯЩ…Ш© Ш¬Ш№Щ„ЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҒЩҠ ЩӮЩ„ЩҲШЁ Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩү ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҒЩҠ ЩӮЩ„ЩҲШЁ Щ…ЩҶ ШҙШ§ШЎ Щ…ЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜЩҮ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ ЩҠШұШӯЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩ…Ш§ШЎвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Suhaib (May Allah be pleased with him) reported that the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "There lived a king before you and he had a court magician. As he (the magician) grew old, he said to the king:
hadiths.body: 'I have grown old, so send me a young boy in order to teach him magic.' The king sent him a young boy to serve the purpose. And on his way (to the magician) the young boy met a monk to whom he listened to and liked it. It became his habit that on his way to the magician, he would meet the monk and sit there and would come to the magician (late). The magician used to beat him because of this delay. He complained about this to the monk who said to him: 'When you feel afraid of the magician, say: Members of my family detained me. And when you fear your family, say: The magician detained me.' It so happened that there came a huge beast and it blocked the way of the people, and the young boy said: 'I will know today whether the magician or the monk is better.' He picked up a stone and said: 'O Allah, if the way of the monk is dearer to You than the way of the magician, bring about death to the animal so that the people be able to move about freely.' He threw that stone at it and killed it and the people began to move about freely. He then came to the monk and told him the story. The monk said: 'Son, today you are superior to me. You have come to a stage where I feel that you would be soon put to a trial, and in case you are put to a trial, do not reveal me.' That young boy began to heal those born blind and the lepers and he, in fact, began to cure people from all kinds of illnesses. When a courtier of the king who had gone blind heard about him, he came to him with numerous gifts and said, 'If you cure me, all these things will be yours.' He said, 'I myself do not cure anyone. It is Allah, the Exalted, Alone Who cures; and if you affirm faith in Allah, I shall also supplicate to Allah to cure you.' This courtier affirmed his faith in Allah and Allah cured him. He came to the king and sat by his side as he used to sit before. The king said to him, 'Who restored your eyesight?' He said, 'My Rubb.' Thereupon he said, 'Do you have another lord besides me?' He said, 'My Rubb and your Rubb is Allah.' So the king kept torturing him untill he revealed the young boy. The young boy was thus summoned and the king said to him, 'O boy, it has been conveyed to me that you have become so much proficient in your magic that you cure the blind and the lepers and you do such and such.' Thereupon he said, 'I do not cure anyone; it is Allah Alone Who cures,' and the king took hold of him and began to torture him until he revealed of the monk. The monk was summoned and it was said to him: 'You should turn back from your religion.' But he refused. The king sent for a saw, placed it in the middle of his head and cut him into two parts that fell down. Then the courtier of the king was brought forward and it was said to him: 'Turn back from your religion.' He, too, refused, and the saw was placed in the midst of his head and he was torn into two parts. Then the boy was sent for and it was said to him: 'Turn back from your religion.' He refused. The king then handed him over to a group of his courtiers, and said to them: 'Take him to such and such mountain; make him climb up that mountain and when you reach its peak ask him to renounce his Faith. If he refuses to do so, push him to his death.' So they took him and made him climb up the mountain and he said: 'O Allah, save me from them in any way you like,' and the mountain began to shake and they all fell down (dead) and that young boy came walking to the king. The king said to him, 'What happened to your companions?' He said, 'Allah has saved me from them.' He again handed him to some of his courtiers and said: 'Take him and carry him in a boat and when you reach the middle of the sea, ask him to renounce his religion. If he does not renounce his religion throw him (into the water).' So they took him and he said: 'O Allah, save me from them.' The boat turned upside down and they all drowned except the young boy who came walking to the king. The king said to him, 'What happened to your companions?'
