hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШІЩҠШҜ Ш№Щ…ШұЩҲ ШЁЩҶ ШЈШ®Ш·ШЁ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШөШ§ШұЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШөЩ„Щү ШЁЩҶШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш§Щ„ЩҒШ¬ШұШҢ ЩҲШөШ№ШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҶШЁШұШҢ ЩҒШ®Ш·ШЁЩҶШ§ ШӯШӘЩү ШӯШ¶ШұШӘ Ш§Щ„ШёЩҮШұШҢ ЩҒЩҶШІЩ„ ЩҒШөЩ„ЩүШҢ Ш«Щ… ШөШ№ШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҶШЁШұ ШӯШӘЩү ШӯШ¶ШұШӘ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШөШұШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩҶШІЩ„ ЩҒШөЩ„ЩүШҢ Ш«Щ… ШөШ№ШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҶШЁШұ ШӯШӘЩү ШәШұШЁШӘ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…ШіШҢ ЩҒШЈШ®ШЁШұЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҮЩҲ ЩғШ§ШҰЩҶШҢ ЩҒШЈШ№Щ„Щ…ЩҶШ§ ШЈШӯЩҒШёЩҶШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: 'Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) said:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "He who vows to obey Allah, should obey Him. But he who vows to disobey Allah, should not disobey Him." [Al- Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш№Ш§ШҰШҙШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҶШ°Шұ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШ·ЩҠШ№ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩ„ЩҠШ·Ш№ЩҮ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩҶШ°Шұ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШ№ШөЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШ№ШөЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Umm Sharik (May Allah be pleased with her) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) ordered me to kill chameleon. He also said, "It blew (fire) on Prophet (п·ә) Ibrahim. [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: -ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈЩ… ШҙШұЩҠЩғ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЈЩ…ШұЩҮШ§ ШЁЩӮШӘЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҲШІШ§Шә ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҠЩҶЩҒШ® Ш№Щ„Щү ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "He who kills a chameleon at the first blow, such and such number of good deeds will be awarded to him; whoever kills it at the second blow, such and such number of merits will be recorded for him. And if he kills it at the third blow, he will get such and such merits." Another narration is: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "If anyone kills a chameleon with the first blow, a hundred good deeds will be recorded for him; less than that will be recorded for him if he kills it at the second blow, and still less rewards will be recorded for him if he kills it at the third blow." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңЩ…ЩҶ ЩӮШӘЩ„ ЩҲШІШәШ© ЩҒЩҠ ШЈЩҲЩ„ Ш¶ШұШЁШ© ЩҒЩ„ЩҮ ЩғШ°Ш§ ЩҲЩғШ°Ш§ ШӯШіЩҶШ©ШҢЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩӮШӘЩ„ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¶ШұШЁШ© Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш§ЩҶЩҠШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩ„ЩҮ ЩғШ°Ш§ ЩҲЩғШ°Ш§ ШӯШіЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҲШҜЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҲЩ„ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ЩӮШӘЩ„ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¶ШұШЁШ© Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш©ШҢ ЩҒЩ„ЩҮ ЩғШ°Ш§ ЩҲЩғШ°Ш§ ШӯШіЩҶШ©вҖқ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩ…ЩҶ ЩӮШӘЩ„ ЩҲШІШәШ§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҠ ШЈЩҲЩ„ Ш¶ШұШЁШ©ШҢ ЩғШӘШЁ Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш§ШҰШ© ШӯШіЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩҒЩү Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш§ЩҶЩҠШ© ШҜЩҲЩҶ Ш°Щ„ЩғШҢ ЩҲЩҒЩү Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш§Щ„Ш«Ш© ШҜЩҲЩҶ Ш°Щ„ЩғвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ШәШ©: В«Ш§Щ„ЩҲШІШәВ» Ш§Щ„Ш№ШёШ§Щ… Щ…ЩҶ ШіШ§Щ… ШЈШЁШұШө.

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: "A man (from amongst the people before you) said: 'Indeed! I will give in charity.' So he took his Sadaqah out and placed it in a thief's hand. In the morning the people were talking (about this incident) and saying: 'Sadaqah was given to a thief last night.' The man said: 'O Allah! Praise be to You. I have given Sadaqah to a thief. Indeed, I will give in charity!' So he took his Sadaqah out and he placed it in a prostitute's hand. In the morning the people were talking (about this incident) and saying: 'Sadaqah was given to a prostitute last night.' On hearing this, the man said: 'Praise be to You, O Allah! I gave Sadaqah to a prostitute. Indeed, I will give in charity!' So he took his Sadaqah out and placed it in a rich man's hand. In the morning the people were talking (about this incident) and saying: 'Sadaqah was given to a rich man last night.' The man said: 'O Allah! Praise be to You (for helping me) give charity to a thief, a prostitute and a rich man.' Then he had a dream in which he was told that his Sadaqah to the thief might result in his refraining from his theft, his Sadaqah to the prostitute might help her abstain from her immorality, and his Sadaqah to the rich man might help him pay heed and spend from what Allah had bestowed upon him." [Al- Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШ¬Щ„ Щ„ШЈШӘШөШҜЩӮЩҶ ШЁШөШҜЩӮШ©ШҢ ЩҒШ®ШұШ¬ ШЁШөШҜЩӮШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҠШҜ ШіШ§ШұЩӮШҢ ЩҒШЈШөШЁШӯЩҲШ§ ЩҠШӘШӯШҜШ«ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӘШөШҜЩӮ Ш№Щ„Щү ШіШ§ШұЩӮвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Щ„ШЈШӘШөШҜЩӮЩҶ ШЁШөШҜЩӮШ©ШҢ ЩҒШ®ШұШ¬ ШЁШөШҜЩӮШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҠШҜ ШІШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©вҖҸШҹвҖҸвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒШЈШөШЁШӯЩҲШ§ ЩҠШӘШӯШҜШ«ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӘШөШҜЩӮ Ш№Щ„Щү ШІШ§ЩҶЩҠШ© ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Ш№Щ„Щү ШІШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©ШҢ Щ„ШЈШӘШөШҜЩӮЩҶ ШЁШөШҜЩӮШ©ШҢ ЩҒШ®ШұШ¬ ШЁШөШҜЩӮШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҠШҜ ШәЩҶЩҠвҖҸ,вҖҸ ЩҒШЈШөШЁШӯЩҲШ§ ЩҠШӘШӯШҜШ«ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ!вҖҸ ШӘШөШҜЩӮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш№Щ„Щү ШәЩҶЩҠвҖҸ,вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Ш№Щ„Щү ШіШ§ШұЩӮ ШҢ ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү ШІШ§ЩҶЩҠШ©ШҢ ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү ШәЩҶЩҠвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒШЈШӘЩү ЩҒЩӮЩҠЩ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ…Ш§ ШөШҜЩӮШӘЩғ Ш№Щ„Щү ШіШ§ШұЩӮШҢ ЩҒЩ„Ш№Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШіШӘШ№ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶ ШіШұЩӮШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҲШЈЩ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШІШ§ЩҶЩҠШ© ЩҒЩ„Ш№Щ„ЩҮШ§ ШӘШіШӘШ№ЩҒ Ш№ЩҶ ШІЩҶШ§ЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲШЈЩ…Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШәЩҶЩҠ ЩҒЩ„Ш№Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШ№ШӘШЁШұШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩҶЩҒЩӮ Щ…Щ…Ш§ ШўШӘШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠ ШЁЩ„ЩҒШёЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ…ШіЩ„Щ… ШЁЩ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Huraira reported:
hadiths.body: Meat was one day brought to the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) and a foreleg was offered to him, a part which he liked. He sliced with his teeth a piece out of it and said: I shall be the leader of mankind on the Day of Resurrection. Do you know why? Allah would gather in one plain the earlier and the later (of the human race) on the Day of Resurrection. Then the voice of the proclaimer would be heard by all of them and the eyesight would penetrate through all of them and the sun would come near. People would then experience a degree of anguish, anxiety and agony which they shall not be able to bear and they shall not be able to stand. Some people would say to the others: Don you see in which trouble you are? Don't you see what (misfortune) has overtaken you? Why don't you find one who should intercede for you with your Lord? Some would say to the others: Go to Adam. And they would go to Adam and say: O Adam, you are the father of mankind. Allah created you by His own Hand and breathed in you of His spirit and ordered the angels to prostrate before you. Intercede for us with your Lord Don't you see in what (trouble) we are? Don't you see what (misfortune) has overtaken us? Adam would say: Verily, my Lord is angry, to an extent to which He had never been angry before nor would He be angry afterward. Verily, He forbade me (to go near) that tree and I disobeyed Him. I am concerned with my own self. Go to someone else; go to Noah. They would come to Noah and would say: O Noah, you are the first of the Messengers (sent) on the earth (after Adam), and Allah named you as a" Grateful Servant," intercede for us with your Lord. Don't you see in what (trouble) we are? Don't you see what (misfortune) has overtaken us? He would say: Verily, my Lord is angry today as He had never been angry before, and would never be angry afterwards. There had emanated a curse from me with which I cursed my people. I am concerned with only myself, I am concerned only with myself; you better go to Ibrahim (peace be upon him). They would go to Ibrahim and say: You are the apostle of Allah and His Friend amongst the inhabitants of the earth; intercede for us with your Lord. Don't you see in which (trouble) we are? Don't you see what (misfortune) has overtaken us? Ibrahim would say to them: Verily, my Lord is today angry as He had never been angry before and would never be angry afterwards. and (Ibrahim) would mention his lies (and then say): I am concerned only with myself, I am concerned only with myself. You better go to someone else: go to Moses. They would come to Moses (peace be upon him) and say: O Moses, you are Allah's messenger, Allah blessed you with His messengership and His conversation amongst people. Intercede for us with your Lord. Don't you see in what (trouble) we are? Don't you see what (misfortune) has overtaken us? Moses (peace be upon him) would say to them: Verily. my Lord is angry as He had never been angry before and would never be angry afterwards. I, in fact, killed a person whom I had not been ordered to kill. I am concerned with myself, I am concerned with myself. You better go to Jesus (peace be upon him). They would come to Jesus and would say: O Jesus, you are the messenger of Allah and you conversed with people in the cradle, (you are) His Word which I-Ie sent down upon Mary. and (you are) the Spirit from Him; so intercede for us with your Lord. Don't you see (the trouble) in which we are? Don't you see (the misfortune) that has overtaken us? Jesus (peace be upon him) would say: Verily, my Lord is angry today as He had never been angry before or would ever be angry afterwards. He mentioned no sin of his. (He simply said: ) I am concerned with myself, I am concerned with myself; you go to someone else: better go to Muhammad (п·ә). They would come to me and say: O Mahammad, you are the messenger of Allah and the last of the apostles. Allah has pardoned you all your previous and later sins. Intercede for us with your Lord; don't you see in which (trouble) we are? Don't you see what (misfortune) has overtaken us? I shall then set off and come below the Throne and fall down prostrate before my Lord; then Allah would reveal to me and inspire me with some of His Praises and Glorifications which He had not revealed to anyone before me. He would then say: Muhammad, raise your head; ask and it would be granted; intercede and intercession would be accepted I would then raise my head and say: O my Lord, my people, my people. It would be said: O Muhammad, bring in by the right gate of Paradise those of your people who would have no account to render. They would share with the people some other door besides this door. The Prophet (п·ә) then said: By Him in Whose Hand is the life of Muhammad, verify the distance between two door leaves of the Paradise is as great as between Mecca and Hajar, or as between Mecca and Busra. [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш№ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠ ШҜШ№ЩҲШ©ШҢ ЩҒШұЩҒШ№ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш°ШұШ§Ш№ШҢ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ШӘШ№Ш¬ШЁЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩҶЩҮШі Щ…ЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩҶЩҮШіШ© ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩҶШ§ ШіЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҠШ§Щ…Ш©ШҢ ЩҮЩ„ ШӘШҜШұЩҲЩҶ Щ…Щ… Ш°Ш§ЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҠШ¬Щ…Ш№ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҲЩ„ЩҠЩҶ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШўШ®ШұЩҠЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ ШөШ№ЩҠШҜ ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЁШөШұЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШёШұШҢ ЩҲЩҠШіЩ…Ш№ЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШҜШ§Ш№ЩҠШҢ ЩҲШӘШҜЩҶЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…ШіШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЁЩ„Шә Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШәЩ… ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩғШұШЁ Щ…Ш§ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШ·ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩҶ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШӯШӘЩ…Щ„ЩҲЩҶ ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШівҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩҲЩҶ ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶШӘЩ… ЩҒЩҠЩҮ ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩ„ШәЩғЩ…ШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҶШёШұЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҠШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩғЩ… ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ ШЁШ№Ш¶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші Щ„ШЁШ№Ш¶вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШЁЩҲЩғЩ… ШўШҜЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶЩҮ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШўШҜЩ… ШЈЩҶШӘ ШЈШЁЩҲ Ш§Щ„ШЁШҙШұШҢ Ш®Щ„ЩӮЩғ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҠШҜЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩҶЩҒШ® ЩҒЩҠЩғ Щ…ЩҶ ШұЩҲШӯЩҮШҢ ЩҲШЈЩ…Шұ Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„Ш§ШҰЩғШ©ШҢ ЩҒШіШ¬ШҜЩҲШ§ Щ„Щғ ЩҲШЈШіЩғЩҶЩғ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ШЁЩ„ШәЩҶШ§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШұШЁЩҠ ШәШ¶ШЁ ШәШ¶ШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ШЁШ№ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩҶЩҮШ§ЩҶЩҠ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШҙШ¬ШұШ©ШҢ ЩҒШ№ШөЩҠШӘШҢ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШәЩҠШұЩҠШҢ ШҘШ°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ЩҶЩҲШӯШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶ ЩҶЩҲШӯШ§ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ЩҶЩҲШӯ ШҢ ШЈЩҶШӘ ШЈЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШұШіЩ„ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ШҢ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШіЩ…Ш§Щғ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШЁШҜШ§ЩӢ ШҙЩғЩҲШұШ§ЩӢШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩ„ШәЩҶШ§ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШұШЁЩҠ ШәШ¶ШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… ШәШ¶ШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҶ ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ШЁШ№ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШҜ ЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ Щ„ЩҠ ШҜШ№ЩҲШ© ШҜШ№ЩҲШӘ ШЁЩҮШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩӮЩҲЩ…ЩҠШҢ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШәЩҠШұЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШЈЩҶШӘ ЩҶШЁЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ®Щ„ЩҠЩ„ЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ Ш§ШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ЩҮЩ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШұШЁЩҠ ШәШ¶ШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… ШәШ¶ШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҶ ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ШЁШ№ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩҠ ЩғЩҶШӘ ЩғШ°ШЁШӘ Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш« ЩғШ°ШЁШ§ШӘШҢ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШәЩҠШұЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…ЩҲШіЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЩҲШіЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Щ…ЩҲШіЩү ШЈЩҶШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒШ¶Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁШұШіШ§Щ„Ш§ШӘЩҮ ЩҲШЁЩғЩ„Ш§Щ…ЩҮ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШіШҢ Ш§ШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШұШЁЩҠ ЩӮШҜ ШәШ¶ШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… ШәШ¶ШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҶ ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ШЁШ№ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩҠ ЩӮШҜ ЩӮШӘЩ„ШӘ ЩҶЩҒШіШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ШЈЩҲЩ…Шұ ШЁЩӮШӘЩ„ЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШәЩҠШұЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш№ЩҠШіЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶ Ш№ЩҠШіЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Ш№ЩҠШіЩү ШЈЩҶШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩғЩ„Щ…ШӘЩҮ ШЈЩ„ЩӮШ§ЩҮШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…ШұЩҠЩ… ЩҲШұЩҲШӯ Щ…ЩҶЩҮ ШҢ ЩҲЩғЩ„Щ…ШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҮШҜ вҖҺШҢ Ш§ШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш№ЩҠШіЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШұШЁЩҠ ШәШ¶ШЁ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… ШәШ¶ШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩҶ ЩҠШәШ¶ШЁ ШЁШ№ШҜЩҮ Щ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ°ЩғШұ Ш°ЩҶШЁШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШәЩҠШұЩҠШҢ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ"вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶЩҠ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜ ШЈЩҶШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШ®Ш§ШӘЩ… Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШЁЩҠШ§ШЎШҢ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШәЩҒШұ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш§ ШӘЩӮШҜЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш°ЩҶШЁЩғ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ШӘШЈШ®ШұШҢ Ш§ШҙЩҒШ№ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩғШҢ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘШұЩү Щ…Ш§ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒШЈЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮШҢ ЩҒШЈШӘЩҠ ШӘШӯШӘ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШұШҙШҢ ЩҒШЈЩӮШ№ ШіШ§Ш¬ШҜШ§ЩӢ Щ„ШұШЁЩҠШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩҠЩҒШӘШӯ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ Щ…ШӯШ§Щ…ШҜЩҮШҢ ЩҲШӯШіЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш«ЩҶШ§ШЎ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢ Щ„Щ… ЩҠЩҒШӘШӯ Ш№Щ„Щү ШЈШӯШҜ ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҠ Ш«Щ… ЩҠЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜ Ш§ШұЩҒШ№ ШұШЈШіЩғШҢ ШіЩ„ ШӘШ№Ш·ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШ§ШҙЩҒШ№ ШӘШҙЩҒШ№ШҢ ЩҒШЈШұЩҒШ№ ШұШЈШіЩҠШҢ ЩҒШЈЩӮЩҲЩ„ ШЈЩ…ШӘЩҠ ЩҠШ§ШұШЁШҢ ШЈЩ…ШӘЩҠ ЩҠШ§ ШұШЁШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜ ШЈШҜШ®Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩ…ШӘЩғ Щ…ЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ШӯШіШ§ШЁ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҠЩ…ЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҲШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ЩҲЩҮЩ… ШҙШұЩғШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҒЩҠЩ…Ш§ ШіЩҲЩү Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШЁЩҲШ§ШЁвҖҸ"вҖҸ Ш«Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲШ§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ШЁЩҠШҜЩҮ ШҘЩҶ Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩҠЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШөШұШ§Ш№ЩҠЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ Щ…ШөШ§ШұЩҠШ№ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШЁЩҠЩҶ Щ…ЩғШ© ЩҲЩҮШ¬ШұШҢ ШЈЩҲ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШЁЩҠЩҶ Щ…ЩғШ© ЩҲШЁШөШұЩүвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: Ibrahim (п·ә) brought his wife and her son Isma'il (п·ә), while she was suckling him, to a place near the Ka'bah under a tree on the spot of Zamzam, at the highest place in the mosque. In those days, there was no human being in Makkah, nor was there any water. So he made them sit over there and placed near them a leather bag containing some dates, and a small water-skin containing some water, and set out homeward. Isma'il's mother followed him saying: "O Ibrahim! Where are you going, leaving us in this valley where there is no person whose company we may enjoy, nor is there anything (to enjoy)?" She repeated that to him many times, but he did not look back at her. Then she asked him: "Has Allah commanded you to do so?" He said: "Yes." She said: "Then He will not neglect us." She returned while Ibrahim proceeded onwards. Having reached the Thaniya, where they could not see him, he faced Ka'bah, raised his both hands and supplicated: "O our Rubb! I have made some of my offspring to dwell in an uncultivable valley by Your Sacred House (the Ka'bah at Makkah) in order, O our Rubb, that they may perform As-Salat (Iqamat-as-Salat). So fill some hearts among men with love towards them, and (O Allah) provide them with fruits so that they may give thanks." (14:37). Isma'il's mother went on suckling Isma'il and drinking from the water which she had. When the water in the water-skin had all been used up, she became thirsty and her child also became thirsty. She started looking at Isma'il, tossing in agony. She left him, for she could not endure looking at him, and found that the mountain of As-Safa was the nearest mountain to her on that land. She stood on it and started looking at the valley keenly so that she might see somebody, but she could not see anybody. Then she descended from As-Safa, and when she reached the valley, she tucked up her robe and ran in the valley like a person in distress and trouble till she crossed the valley and reached Al-Marwah mountain where she stood and started looking, expecting to see somebody, but she could not see anybody. She repeated that (running between As-Safa and Al-Marwah) seven times." Ibn 'Abbas further related: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "This is the source of the tradition of the Sa'y - i.e., the going of people between the two mountains. When she reached Al-Marwah (for the last time), she heard a voice and she exclaimed: 'Shshs!' (Silencing herself) and listened attentively. She heard the voice again and said: 'O (whoever you may be) You have made me hear your voice; have you any succour for me?' And behold! She saw an angel at the place of Zamzam, digging the earth with his heel (or with his wing), till water flowed out from that place. She started to make something like of a basin around it, using her hands in this way and began to fill her water- skin with water with her hands, and the water was flowing out until she had scooped some of it." The Prophet (п·ә) further said, "May Allah bestow mercy on Isma'il's mother! Had she let the Zamzam flow without trying to control it (or had she not scooped in that water) while filling her water-skin, Zamzam would have been a stream flowing on the surface of the earth." The Prophet (п·ә) further added, "Then she drank (water) and suckled her child. The angel said to her: 'Do not be afraid of being neglected, for this is the site on which the House of Allah will be built by this boy and his father, and Allah will never let neglected His people.' The House of Allah (the Ka'bah) at that time was on a high place resembling a hillock, and when torrents came, they flowed to its right and left. She continued living in that way till some people from the tribe of Jurhum passed by her and her child. As they were coming from through the way of Kada', in the lower part of Makkah where they saw a bird that had a habit of flying around water and not leaving it. They said: 'This bird must be flying over water, though we know that there is no water in this valley.' They sent one or two messengers who discovered the source of water, and returned to inform them of the water. So, they all came towards the water." The Prophet (п·ә) added, "Isma'il's mother was sitting near the water. They asked her: 'Do you allow us to stay with you?' She replied: 'Yes, but you will have no right to possess the water.' They agreed to that." The Prophet (п·ә) further said, "Isma'il's mother was pleased with the whole situation as she used to love the company of the people. So, they settled there, and later on they sent for their families who came and settled with them. The child (i.e., Isma'il) grew up and learnt Arabic from them (his virtues) caused them to love and admire him as he grew up, and when he reached the age of puberty, they gave him one of their daughters in marriage. After Isma'il's mother had died, Ibrahim came after Isma'il's marriage in order to see his family that he had left before, but he did not find Isma'il there. When he asked Isma'il's wife about him, she replied: 'He has gone in search of our livelihood.' Then he asked her about their way of living and their condition, and she replied complaining to him: 'We are living in hardship, misery and destitution.' He said: 'When your husband returns, convey my salutations to him and tell him to change the threshold of the door of his house.' When Isma'il came, he seemed to have perceived something unusual. He asked his wife: 'Did anyone visit you?' She replied: 'Yes, an old man of such and such description came and asked me about you and I informed him, and he asked about our state of living, and, I told him that we were living in hardship and poverty.' Thereupon Isma'il said: 'Did he advise you anything?' She replied: 'Yes, he told me to convey his salutations to you and to change the threshold of your door.' Isma'il said: 'That was my father, and he has ordered me to divorce you. Go back to your family.' So Isma'il divorced her and married another woman from amongst them (Jurhum). Then Ibrahim stayed away from them for a period as long as Allah wished, and called on them again but did not find Isma'il. So he came to Isma'il's wife and asked her about him. She said: 'He has gone in search of our livelihood.' Ibrahim asked her about their sustenance and living: 'How are you getting on?' She replied: 'We are prosperous and well off.' Then she praised Allah, the Exalted. Ibrahim asked: 'What kind of food do you eat?' She said: 'Meat.' He said: 'What do you drink?' She said: 'Water.' He said, 'O Allah! Bless their meat and water!"' The Prophet (п·ә) added, "At that time they did not have grain, and if they had grain, he would have also invoked Allah to bless it." The Prophet (п·ә) further said, "If somebody has only these two things as his sustenance, his health and disposition will be badly affected because these things do not suit him unless he lives in Makkah." The Prophet (п·ә) added, "Then Ibrahim said to Isma'il's wife, 'When your husband comes, give my regards to him and tell him that he should keep firm the threshold of his door.' When Isma'il came back, he asked his wife: 'Did anyone call on you?' She replied: 'Yes, a good looking old man came to me.' She praised him and added: 'He asked about you, and I informed him, and he asked about our livelihood and I told him that we were in good condition.' Isma'il asked her: 'Did he give you a piece of advice?' She said: 'Yes, he told me to convey his regards to you and ordered that you should keep firm the threshold of your door.' On that Isma'il said: 'He was my father and you are the threshold of the door. He has ordered me to keep you with me.' Then Ibrahim stayed away from them for a period as long as Allah wished and called on them afterwards. He saw Isma'il under a tree near Zamzam, sharpening his arrows. When he saw Ibrahim, he rose up to welcome him, and they greeted each other as a father does with his son or a son does with his father. Ibrahim said: 'O Isma'il! Allah has given me an order.' Isma'il said: 'Do what your Rubb has commanded you to do.' Ibrahim asked: 'Will you help me?' Isma'il said: 'I will help you.' Ibrahim said: 'Allah has ordered me to build a house here, pointing to a hillock higher than the land surrounding it."' The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) added, "Then they raised the foundations of the House (i.e., Ka'bah). Isma'il brought the stones and Ibrahim was building (the house). When the walls became high, Isma'il brought stone and placed it for Ibrahim who stood over it and carried on building the House, while Isma'il was handing over the stones to him, both of them prayed: 'O our Rubb! Accept this service from us! Verily, You are the All- Hearer and the All-Knower."' [Al-Bukhari]. There are some more narrations about this incident, some adding details and some with minor variations in the wordings.
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЁШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҲШЁШ§ШЁЩҶЩҮШ§ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҲЩҮЩҠ ШӘШұШ¶Ш№ЩҮ ШӯШӘЩү ЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮШ§ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҠШӘ Ш№ЩҶШҜ ШҜЩҲШӯШ© ЩҒЩҲЩӮ ШІЩ…ШІЩ… ЩҒЩҠ ШЈШ№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіШ¬ШҜ ЩҲЩ„ЩҠШі ШЁЩ…ЩғШ© ЩҠЩҲЩ…ШҰШ° ШЈШӯШҜ ЩҲЩ„ЩҠШі ШЁЩҮШ§ Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҮЩҶШ§ЩғШҢ ЩҲЩҲШ¶Ш№ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩҮЩ…Ш§ Ш¬ШұШ§ШЁШ§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҠЩҮ ШӘЩ…ШұШҢ ЩҲШіЩӮШ§ШЎ ЩҒЩҠЩҮ Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩӮЩҒЩү ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… Щ…ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒШӘШЁШ№ШӘЩҮ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШЈЩҠЩҶ ШӘШ°ЩҮШЁ ЩҲШӘШӘШұЩғЩҶШ§ ШЁЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ Щ„ЩҠШі ЩҒЩҠЩҮ ШЈЩҶЩҠШі ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШҙЩҠШЎвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Щ„ЩҮ Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ…ШұШ§ШұШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШ¬Ш№Щ„ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠЩ„ШӘЩҒШӘ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШўЩ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩ…ШұЩғ ШЁЩҮШ°Ш§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ… ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘШ°Ш§ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШ¶ЩҠШ№ЩҶШ§ШҢ Ш«Щ… ШұШ¬Ш№ШӘШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ„Ш«ЩҶЩҠШ© ШӯЩҠШ« ЩҠШұЩҲЩҶЩҮ Ш§ШіШӘЩӮШЁЩ„ ШЁЩҲШ¬ЩҮЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҠШӘ ШҢ Ш«Щ… ШҜШ№Ш§ ШЁЩҮШӨЩ„Ш§ШЎ Ш§Щ„ШҜШ№ЩҲШ§ШӘ ШҢ ЩҒШұЩҒШ№ ЩҠШҜЩҠЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸШұШЁЩҶШ§ ШҘЩҶЩҠ ШЈШіЩғЩҶШӘ Щ…ЩҶ Ш°ШұЩҠШӘЩҠ ШЁЩҲШ§ШҜ ШәЩҠШұ Ш°ЩҠ ШІШұШ№вҖҸ}вҖҸ ШӯШӘЩү ШЁЩ„Шә вҖҸ{вҖҸЩҠШҙЩғШұЩҲЩҶвҖҸ}вҖҸ ЩҲШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ШӘШұШ¶Ш№ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ ЩҲШӘШҙШұШЁ Щ…ЩҶ Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҶЩҒШҜ Щ…Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩӮШ§ШЎ Ш№Ш·ШҙШӘШҢ ЩҲШ№Ш·Шҙ Ш§ШЁЩҶЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШӘЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҠШӘЩ„ЩҲЩү -ШЈЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШӘЩ„ШЁШ·- ЩҒШ§ЩҶШ·Щ„ЩӮШӘ ЩғШұШ§ЩҮЩҠШ© ШЈЩҶ ШӘЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШҢ ЩҒЩҲШ¬ШҜШӘ Ш§Щ„ШөЩҒШ§ ШЈЩӮШұШЁ Ш¬ШЁЩ„ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ЩҠЩ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ…ШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШҢ Ш«Щ… Ш§ШіШӘЩӮШЁЩ„ШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠ ШӘЩҶШёШұ ЩҮЩ„ ШӘШұЩү ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ШӘШұ ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩҮШЁШ·ШӘ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШөЩҒШ§ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ШЁЩ„ШәШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШҢ ШұЩҒШ№ШӘ Ш·ШұЩҒ ШҜШұШ№ЩҮШ§ШҢ Ш«Щ… ШіШ№ШӘ ШіШ№ЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШҘЩҶШіШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш¬ЩҮЩҲШҜ ШӯШӘЩү Ш¬Ш§ЩҲШІШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШҢ Ш«Щ… ШЈШӘШӘ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұЩҲШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ…ШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ ЩҮЩ„ ШӘШұЩү ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ШӘШұ ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ ШіШЁШ№ Щ…ШұШ§ШӘШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҒШ°Щ„Щғ ШіШ№ЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮШ§вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ШЈШҙШұЩҒШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұЩҲШ© ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШөЩҲШӘШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШөЩҮ-ШӘШұЩҠШҜ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҮШ§- Ш«Щ… ШӘШіЩ…Щ‘Ш№ШӘ ШҢ ЩҒШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШЈЩҠШ¶Ш§ЩӢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШҜ ШЈШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШҘЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩғ ШәЩҲШ§Ш« ЩҒШЈШәШ«ШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҮЩү ШЁШ§Щ„Щ…Щ„Щғ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Щ…ЩҲШ¶Ш№ ШІЩ…ШІЩ…ШҢ ЩҒШЁШӯШ« ШЁШ№ЩӮШЁЩҮ -ШЈЩҲ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШЁШ¬ЩҶШ§ШӯЩҮ- ШӯШӘЩү ШёЩҮШұ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШӘШӯЩҲШ¶ЩҮ ЩҲШӘЩӮЩҲЩ„ ШЁЩҠШҜЩҮШ§ ЩҮЩғШ°Ш§ШҢ ЩҲШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШӘШәШұЩҒ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎ ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩӮШ§ШҰЩҮШ§ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ЩҠЩҒЩҲШұ ШЁШ№ШҜЩ…Ш§ ШӘШәШұЩҒШҢ ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЁЩӮШҜШұ Щ…Ш§ ШӘШәШұЩҒШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШұШӯЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ Щ„ЩҲ ШӘШұЩғШӘ ШІЩ…ШІЩ… -ШЈЩҲ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ Щ„Щ… ШӘШәШұЩҒ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ Щ„ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШІЩ…ШІЩ… Ш№ЩҠЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш№ЩҠЩҶШ§вҖқ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШҙШұШЁШӘ ШҢ ЩҲШЈШұШ¶Ш№ШӘ ЩҲЩ„ШҜЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„ЩғвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Ш§ ШӘШ®Ш§ЩҒЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Ш¶ЩҠШ№Ш© ЩҒШҘЩҶ ЩҮЩҮЩҶШ§ ШЁЩҠШӘШ§ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҠШЁЩҶЩҠЩҮ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШәЩ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲШЈШЁЩҲЩҮШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШ¶ЩҠШ№ ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҠШӘ Щ…ШұШӘЩҒШ№Ш§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ЩғШ§Щ„ШұШ§ШЁЩҠШ© ШӘШЈШӘЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҠЩҲЩ„ШҢ ЩҒШӘШЈШ®Ш° Ш№ЩҶ ЩҠЩ…ЩҠЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШҙЩ…Ш§Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶШӘ ЩғШ°Щ„Щғ ШӯШӘЩү Щ…ШұШӘ ШЁЩҮЩ… ШұЩҒЩӮШ© Щ…ЩҶ Ш¬ШұЩҮЩ…ШҢ ШЈЩҲ ШЈЩҮЩ„ ШЁЩҠШӘ Щ…ЩҶ Ш¬ШұЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩӮШЁЩ„ЩҠЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ Ш·ШұЩҠЩӮ ЩғШҜШ§ШЎШҢ ЩҒЩҶШІЩ„ЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҠ ШЈШіЩҒЩ„ Щ…ЩғШ©ШҢ ЩҒШұШЈЩҲШ§ Ш·Ш§ШҰШұШ§ЩӢ Ш№Ш§ШҰЩҒШ§ЩӢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„Ш·Ш§ШҰШұ Щ„ЩҠШҜЩҲШұ Ш№Щ„Щү Щ…Ш§ШЎ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ Ш№ЩҶШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШӘШЈШ°ЩҶЩҠЩҶ Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШЈЩҶ ЩҶЩҶШІЩ„ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩҶ Щ„Ш§ ШӯЩӮ Щ„ЩғЩ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§ШівҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҒШЈЩ„ЩҒЩү Ш°Щ„Щғ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩҠ ШӘШӯШЁ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШіШҢ ЩҒЩҶШІЩ„ЩҲШ§ШҢ ЩҒШЈШұШіЩ„ЩҲШ§ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҶШІЩ„ЩҲШ§ Щ…Ш№ЩҮЩ…ШҢ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҮЩ„ ШЈШЁЩҠШ§ШӘШҢ ЩҲШҙШЁ Ш§Щ„ШәЩ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲШӘШ№Щ„Щ… Ш§Щ„Ш№ШұШЁЩҠЩҮ Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ… ЩҲШЈЩҶЩҒШіЩҮЩ… ЩҲШЈШ№Ш¬ШЁЩҮЩ… ШӯЩҠЩҶ ШҙШЁ ШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ШЈШҜШұЩғШҢ ШІЩҲШ¬ЩҲЩҮ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШ© Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ШӘШӘ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш§ШЎ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШЁШ№ШҜ Щ…Ш§ ШӘШІЩҲШ¬ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҠШ·Ш§Щ„Ш№ ШӘШұЩғШӘЩҮ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ¬ШҜ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ ЩҒШіШЈЩ„ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш®ШұШ¬ ЩҠШЁШӘШәЩҠ Щ„ЩҶШ§- ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШөЩҠШҜ Щ„ЩҶШ§ - Ш«Щ… ШіШЈЩ„ЩҮШ§ Ш№ЩҶ Ш№ЩҠШҙЩҮЩ… ЩҲЩҮЩҠШҰШӘЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ШЁШҙШұ ШҢ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ Ш¶ЩҠЩӮ ЩҲШҙШҜШ©ШҢ ЩҲШҙЩғШӘ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШІЩҲШ¬ЩғШҢ ШЈЩӮШұШҰЩҠ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШәЩҠШұ Ш№ШӘШЁШ© ШЁШ§ШЁЩғШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш°Ш§Щғ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҶЩҠ ШЈЩҶ ШЈЩҒШ§ШұЩӮЩғШҢ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩӮЩҠ ШЁШЈЩҮЩ„ЩғвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒШ·Щ„ЩӮЩҮШ§ ШҢ ЩҲШӘШІЩҲШ¬ Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ… ШЈШ®ШұЩүШҢ ЩҒЩ„ШЁШ« Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ… ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… Щ…Ш§ ШҙШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш«Щ… ШЈШӘШ§ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ№ШҜШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ¬ШҜЩҮ ШҢ ЩҒШҜШ®Щ„ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩҮШҢ ЩҒШіШЈЩ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш®ШұШ¬ ЩҠШЁШӘШәЩҠ Щ„ЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҠЩҒ ШЈЩҶШӘЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҲШіШЈЩ„ЩҮШ§ Ш№ЩҶ Ш№ЩҠШҙЩҮЩ… ЩҲЩҮЩҠШҰШӘЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШӯЩҶ ШЁШ®ЩҠШұ ЩҲШіШ№Ш© ЩҲШЈШ«ЩҶШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…Ш§ Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ…ЩғЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ШӯЩ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ ШҙШұШ§ШЁЩғЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ§ШұЩғ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ШӯЩ… ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩғЩҶ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҠШӨЩ…ШҰШ° ШӯШЁ ЩҲЩ„ЩҲ ЩғШ§ЩҶ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШҜШ№Ш§ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҠЩҮвҖқ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩҮЩ…Ш§ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШ®Щ„ЩҲ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈШӯШҜ ШЁШәЩҠШұ Щ…ЩғШ© ШҘЩ„Ш§ Щ„Щ… ЩҠЩҲШ§ЩҒЩӮШ§ЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© ЩҒШ¬Ш§ШЎ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩҠЩҶ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш°ЩҮШЁ ЩҠШөЩҠШҜШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҶШІЩ„ШҢ ЩҒШӘШ·Ш№Щ… ЩҲШӘШҙШұШЁвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ…ЩғЩ… ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ШҙШұШ§ШЁЩғЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ…ЩҶШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҢ ЩҲШҙШұШ§ШЁЩҶШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ§ШұЩғ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ…ЩҮЩ… ЩҲШҙШұШ§ШЁЩҮЩ…- ЩӮШ§Щ„ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШЈШЁЩҲШ§Щ„ЩӮШ§ШіЩ… ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШЁШұЩғШ© ШҜШ№ЩҲШ© ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШІЩҲШ¬Щғ ШҢ ЩҒШ§ЩӮШұШҰЩҠ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲЩ…ШұЩҠЩҮ ЩҠШ«ШЁШӘ Ш№ШӘШЁШ© ШЁШ§ШЁЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩ„ ШЈШӘШ§ЩғЩ… Щ…ЩҶ ШЈШӯШҜвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ШЈШӘШ§ЩҶШ§ ШҙЩҠШ® ШӯШіЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҮЩҠШҰШ©ШҢ ЩҲШЈШ«ЩҶШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҒШіШЈЩ„ЩҶЩҠ Ш№ЩҶЩғШҢ ЩҒШЈШ®ШЁШұШӘЩҮ ШҢЩҒШіШЈЩ„ЩҶЩҠ ЩғЩҠЩҒ Ш№ЩҠШҙЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩҒШЈШ®ШЁШұШӘЩҮШҢ ШЈЩҶЩ‘Ш§ ШЁШ®ЩҠШұШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШЈЩҲШөШ§Щғ ШЁШҙЩҠШЎвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ЩҠЩӮШұШЈ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…ШҢ ЩҲЩҠШЈЩ…ШұЩғ ШЈЩҶ ШӘШ«ШЁШӘ Ш№ШЁШӘШ© ШЁШ§ШЁЩғШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш°Ш§Щғ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҲШЈЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШӘШЁШ© ШЈЩ…ШұЩҶЩҠ ШЈЩҶ ШЈЩ…ШіЩғЩғШҢ Ш«Щ… Щ„ШЁШ« Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ… Щ…Ш§ ШҙШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ Ш«Щ… Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш°Щ„Щғ ЩҲШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҠШЁШұЩҠ ЩҶШЁЩ„Ш§ЩӢ Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШӯШӘ ШҜЩҲШӯШ© ЩӮШұЩҠШЁШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ ШІЩ…ШІЩ…ШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ШұШўЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ… ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШҢ ЩҒШөЩҶШ№ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ЩҠШөЩҶШ№ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§Щ„ШҜ ШЁШ§Щ„ЩҲЩ„ШҜШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩҲЩ„ШҜ ШЁШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§Щ„ШҜ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩҠШ§ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҶЩҠ ШЁШЈЩ…Шұ ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ§ШөЩҶШ№ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШұШ§Щғ ШұШЁЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШӘШ№ЩҠЩҶЩҶЩҠШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШЈШ№ЩҠЩҶЩғШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЈЩ…ШұЩҶЩҠ ШЈЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҶЩҠ ШЁЩҠШӘШ§ ЩҮЩҮЩҶШ§ШҢ ЩҲШЈШҙШ§Шұ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈЩғЩ…Ш© Щ…ШұШӘЩҒШ№Ш© Ш№Щ„Щү Щ…Ш§ ШӯЩҲЩ„ЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒШ№ЩҶШҜ Ш°Щ„Щғ ШұЩҒШ№ Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҲШ§Ш№ШҜ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҠШӘШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш№Щ„ ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҠШЈШӘЩҠ ШЁШ§Щ„ШӯШ¬Ш§ШұШ© ЩҲШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ЩҠШЁЩҶЩҠ ШӯШӘЩү ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш§ШұШӘЩҒШ№ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҶШ§ШЎШҢ Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШЁЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ШӯШ¬Шұ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮ Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ… Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ЩҠШЁЩҶЩҠ ЩҲШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҠЩҶШ§ЩҲЩ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШӯШ¬Ш§ШұШ© ЩҲЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩҶ вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШұШЁЩҶШ§ ШӘЩӮШЁЩ„ Щ…ЩҶШ§ ШҘЩҶЩғ ШЈЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…ЩҠШ№ Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩ…вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… Ш®ШұШ¬ ШЁШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ЩҲШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ Щ…Ш№ЩҮЩ… ШҙЩҶШ© ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ Щ…Ш§ШЎШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ШӘШҙШұШЁ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШҜШұ Щ„ШЁЩҶЩҮШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү ШөШЁЩҠЩҮШ§ ШӯШӘЩү ЩӮШҜЩ… Щ…ЩғШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҲШ¶Ш№ЩҮШ§ ШӘШӯШӘ ШҜЩҲШӯШ©ШҢ Ш«Щ… ШұШ¬Ш№ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҒШ§ШӘШЁШ№ШӘЩҮ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ ШӯШӘЩү Щ„Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩ„ШәЩҲШ§ ЩғШҜШ§ШЎШҢ ЩҶШ§ШҜШӘЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҲШұШ§ШҰЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШҘЩ„Щү Щ…ЩҶ ШӘШӘШұЩғЩҶШ§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШұШ¶ЩҠШӘ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҒШұШ¬Ш№ШӘШҢ ЩҲШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШӘШҙШұШЁ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩҠШҜШұ Щ„ШЁЩҶЩҮШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү ШөШЁЩҠЩҮШ§ ШӯШӘЩү Щ„Щ…Ш§ ЩҒЩҶЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ Ш°ЩҮШЁШӘ ШҢ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ Щ„Ш№Щ„ЩҠ ШЈШӯШі ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШ°ЩҮШЁШӘ ЩҒШөШ№ШҜШӘ Ш§Щ„ШөЩҒШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ ЩҲЩҶШёШұШӘ ЩҮЩ„ ШӘШӯШі ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ…Ш§ ШЁЩ„ШәШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ШҜЩҠШҢ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘШҢ ЩҲШЈШӘШӘ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұЩҲШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩҒШ№Щ„ШӘ Ш°Щ„Щғ ШЈШҙЩҲШ§Ш·Ш§ШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ Ш°ЩҮШЁШӘ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ Щ…Ш§ ЩҒШ№Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШөШЁЩҠШҢ ЩҒШ°ЩҮШЁШӘ ЩҲЩҶШёШұШӘШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҮЩҲ Ш№Щ„Щү ШӯШ§Щ„ЩҮ ЩғШЈЩҶЩҮ ЩҠЩҶШҙШә Щ„Щ„Щ…ЩҲШӘШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ШӘЩӮШұЩҮШ§ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҮШ§ ШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ Ш°ЩҮШЁШӘ ШҢ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ Щ„Ш№Щ„ЩҠ ШЈШӯШі ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒШ°ЩҮШЁШӘ ЩҒШөШ№ШҜШӘ Ш§Щ„ШөЩҒШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ ЩҲЩҶШёШұШӘШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ШӘШӯШі ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢ ШӯШӘЩү ШЈШӘЩ…ШӘ ШіШЁШ№Ш§ЩӢШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ЩҲ Ш°ЩҮШЁШӘ ШҢ ЩҒЩҶШёШұШӘ Щ…Ш§ ЩҒШ№Щ„ШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҮЩҠ ШЁШөЩҲШӘ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШәШ« ШҘЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш№ЩҶШҜЩғ Ш®ЩҠШұШҢ ЩҒШҘШ°Ш§ Ш¬ШЁШұЩҠЩ„ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШЁШ№ЩӮШЁЩҮ ЩҮЩғШ°Ш§ШҢ ЩҲШәЩ…ШІ ШЁШ№ЩӮШЁЩҮ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ЩҒШ§ЩҶШЁШ«ЩӮ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎ ЩҒШҜЩҮШҙШӘ ШЈЩ… ШҘШіЩ…Ш§Ш№ЩҠЩ„ШҢ ЩҒШ¬Ш№Щ„ШӘ ШӘШӯЩҒЩҶШҢ ЩҲШ°ЩғШұ Ш§Щ„ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШЁШ·ЩҲЩ„ЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ ШЁЩҮШ°ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ§ШӘ ЩғЩ„ЩҮШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ В«Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҲШӯШ©В» Ш§Щ„ШҙШ¬ШұШ© Ш§Щ„ЩғШЁЩҠШұШ©. ЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҮ: В«ЩӮЩҒЩүВ» : ШЈЩҠ: ЩҲЩ„Щү. ... В«ЩҲШ§Щ„Ш¬ШұЩҠВ» : Ш§Щ„ШұШіЩҲЩ„. В«ЩҲШЈЩ„ЩҒЩүВ» : Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ЩҮ ЩҲШ¬ШҜ. ЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҮ: В«ЩҠЩҶШҙШәВ» : ШЈЩҠ: ЩҠШҙЩҮЩӮ.

hadiths.narrator: Sa'id bin Zaid (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying, "Truffle (edible fungus) is a species of Al-Manna and their water cures eye diseases." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ ШЁЩҶ ШІЩҠШҜ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШ§Щ„ЩғЩ…ШЈШ© Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ШӨЩҮШ§ ШҙЩҒШ§ШЎ Щ„Щ„Ш№ЩҠЩҶвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Al-Agharr Al-Muzani (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Sometimes I perceive a veil over my heart, and I supplicate Allah for forgiveness a hundred times in a day." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШәШұ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШІЩҶЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶЩҮ Щ„ЩҠШәШ§ЩҶ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩӮЩ„ШЁЩҠШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶЩҠ Щ„ШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… Щ…Ш§ШҰШ© Щ…ШұШ©вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying, "I swear by Allah that I seek Allah's Pardon and turn to Him in repentance more than seventy times a day." [Al-Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ вҖңЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҘЩҶЩҠ Щ„ШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШЈШӘЩҲШЁ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҠЩҲЩ… ШЈЩғШ«Шұ Щ…ЩҶ ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ Щ…ШұШ©вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "By the One in Whose Hand my soul is! If you do not commit sins, Allah would replace you with a people who would commit sins and seek forgiveness from Allah; and Allah will certainly forgive them." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶЩҮ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩҲШ§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ШЁЩҠШҜЩҮ Щ„ЩҲ Щ„Щ… ШӘШ°ЩҶШЁЩҲШ§ШҢ Щ„Ш°ЩҮШЁ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ШЁЩғЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш¬Ш§ШЎ ШЁЩӮЩҲЩ… ЩҠШ°ЩҶШЁЩҲЩҶ ЩҒЩҠШіШӘШәЩҒШұЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ЩҒЩҠШәЩҒШұ Щ„ЩҮЩ…вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) said:
hadiths.body: We counted Messenger's saying a hundred times during one single sitting: Rabb- ighfir li, wa tubb 'alayya, innaka Antat-Tawwabur-Rahim. (My Rubb! Forgive me and pardon me. Indeed, You are the Oft-Returning with compassion and Ever Merciful." [Abu Dawud and At- Tirmidhi].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…Шұ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҶШ§ ЩҶШ№ШҜ Щ„ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш¬Щ„Ші Ш§Щ„ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜ Щ…Ш§ШҰШ© Щ…ШұШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШұШЁ Ш§ШәЩҒШұ Щ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҲШӘШЁ Ш№Щ„Щү ШҘЩҶЩғ ШЈЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„ШӘЩҲШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„ШұШӯЩҠЩ…вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„ : (( ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶ ШөШӯЩҠШӯ ШәШұЩҠШЁ )) .

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "If anyone constantly seeks pardon (from Allah), Allah will appoint for him a way out of every distress and a relief from every anxiety, and will provide sustenance for him from where he expects not." [Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңЩ…ЩҶ Щ„ШІЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіШӘШәЩҒШ§Шұ ШҢ Ш¬Ш№Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„ЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ЩғЩ„ Ш¶ЩҠЩӮ Щ…Ш®ШұШ¬Ш§ЩӢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩғЩ„ ЩҮЩ… ЩҒШұШ¬Ш§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШұШІЩӮЩҮ Щ…ЩҶ ШӯЩҠШ« Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШӯШӘШіШЁвҖҸ"вҖҸ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜвҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ibn Mas'ud (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "He who says: 'Astaghfir ullah-alladhi la ilaha illa Huwal-Haiyul-Qayyumu, wa atubu ilaihi (I seek the forgiveness of Allah, there is no true god except Allah, the Ever-Living, the Self- Subsisting, and I turn to Him in repentance),' his sins will be forgiven even if he should have run away from the battlefield (while he was engaged in fighting for the Cause of Allah)." [Abu Dawud, At-Tirmidhi and Al-Hakim (on conditions of Al-Bukhari and Muslim for accepting Hadith)].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Щ…ШіШ№ЩҲШҜ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩ…ЩҶ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ Щ„Ш§ ШҘЩ„ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩҮЩҲ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩҠЩҲЩ… ЩҲШЈШӘЩҲШЁ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШҢ ШәЩҒШұШӘ Ш°ЩҶЩҲШЁЩҮ ШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩӮШҜ ЩҒШұ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШІШӯЩҒвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӯШ§ЩғЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШөШӯЩҠШӯ Ш№Щ„Щү ШҙШұШ· Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠ ЩҲЩ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Shaddad bin Aus (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The best supplication for seeking forgiveness (Syed-ul- Istighfar) is to say: 'Allahumma Anta Rabbi, la ilaha illa Anta, khalaqtani wa ana 'abduka, wa ana 'ala 'ahdika wa wa'dika mastata'tu, a'udhu bika min sharri ma sana'tu, abu'u laka bini'matika 'alayya, wa abu'u bidhanbi faghfir li, fa innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa Anta. (O Allah! You are my Rubb. There is no true god except You. You have created me, and I am Your slave, and I hold to Your Covenant as far as I can. I seek refuge in You from the evil of what I have done. I acknowledge the favours that You have bestowed upon me, and I confess my sins. Pardon me, for none but You has the power to pardon).' He who supplicates in these terms during the day with firm belief in it and dies on the same day (before the evening), he will be one of the dwellers of Jannah; and if anyone supplicates in these terms during the night with firm belief in it and dies before the morning, he will be one of the dwellers of Jannah." [Al-Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШҙШҜШ§ШҜ ШЁЩҶ ШЈЩҲШі ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШіЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„ШҘШіШӘШәЩҒШ§Шұ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШЁШҜ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЈЩҶШӘ ШұШЁЩҠШҢ Щ„Ш§ ШҘЩ„ЩҮ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШЈЩҶШӘ ШҢ Ш®Щ„ЩӮШӘЩҶЩҠ ЩҲШЈЩҶШ§ Ш№ШЁШҜЩғШҢ ЩҲШЈЩҶШ§ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш№ЩҮШҜЩғ ЩҲЩҲШ№ШҜЩғ Щ…Ш§ Ш§ШіШӘШ·Ш№ШӘ ШЈШ№ЩҲШ° ШЁЩғ Щ…ЩҶ ШҙШұ Щ…Ш§ ШөЩҶШ№ШӘ ШЈШЁЩҲШЎ Щ„Щғ ШЁЩҶШ№Щ…ШӘЩғ Ш№Щ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҲШЈШЁЩҲШЎ ШЁШ°ЩҶШЁЩҠШҢ ЩҒШ§ШәЩҒШұ Щ„ЩҠ ЩҒШҘЩҶЩҮ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҶЩҲШЁ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ШЈЩҶШӘШҢ Щ…ЩҶ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҮШ§Шұ Щ…ЩҲЩӮЩҶШ§ ШЁЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ШӘ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҠЩҲЩ…ЩҮ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩ…ШіЩҠШҢ ЩҒЩҮЩҲ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ Щ…ЩҲЩӮЩҶ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ШӘ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШөШЁШӯШҢ ЩҒЩҮЩҲ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©вҖқ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ (1)

hadiths.narrator: Thauban (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: Whenever the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) finished his Salat (prayer), he would beg forgiveness three times [by saying, 'Astaghfirullah' (3 times)] and then he would say: "Allahumma Antas-Salamu, wa minkas-Salamu, tabarakta ya Dhal-Jalali wal-Ikram. (O Allah! You are the Bestower of security and security comes from You; Blessed are You. O Possessor of glory and honour)." Imam Al-Auza'i (one of the subnarrators) of this Hadith was asked: "How forgiveness should be sought?" He replied: "I say: Astaghfirullah, Astaghfirullah (I seek forgiveness from Allah. I seek forgiveness from Allah)." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: - ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш«ЩҲШЁШ§ЩҶ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҘШ°Ш§ Ш§ЩҶШөШұЩҒ Щ…ЩҶ ШөЩ„Ш§ШӘЩҮ ШҢ Ш§ШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш§ЩӢ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЈЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…ШҢ ЩҲЩ…ЩҶЩғ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ„Ш§Щ…ШҢ ШӘШЁШ§ШұЩғШӘ ЩҠШ§ ЩҠШ§Ш°Ш§ Ш§Щ„Ш¬Щ„Ш§Щ„ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШҘЩғШұШ§Щ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮЩҠЩ„ Щ„Щ„ШЈЩҲШІШ§Ш№ЩҠ- ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ШЈШӯШҜ ШұЩҲШ§ШӘЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҠЩҒ Ш§Щ„ШҘШіШӘШәЩҒШ§ШұвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШҢ ШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: 'Aishah (May Allah be pleased with her) reported:
hadiths.body: Prior to his demise, the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) used to supplicate frequently: Subhan Allahi wa bihamdihi; Astaghfirullaha wa atubu ilaihi (Allah is free from imperfection, and I begin with praising Him. I beg forgiveness from Allah and I turn to Him in repentance." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш№Ш§ШҰШҙШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩғШ«Шұ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ ЩӮШЁЩ„ Щ…ЩҲШӘЩҮ вҖҸ вҖҸШіШЁШӯШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШЁШӯЩ…ШҜЩҮШҢ ШЈШіШӘШәЩҒШұ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲШЈШӘЩҲШЁ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: I heard the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) saying, "Allah, the Exalted, has said: 'O son of Adam! I shall go on forgiving you so long as you pray to Me and aspire for My forgiveness whatever may be your sins. O son of Adam! I do not care even if your sins should pile up to the sky and should you beg pardon of Me, I would forgive you. O son of Adam! If you come to Me with an earthful of sins and meet Me, not associating anything with Me in worship, I will certainly grant you as much pardon as will fill the earth."' [At-Tirmidhi].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈЩҶШі ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ Ш§ШЁЩҶ ШўШҜЩ… ШҘЩҶЩғ Щ…Ш§ ШҜШ№ЩҲШӘЩҶЩҠ ЩҲШұШ¬ЩҲШӘЩҶЩҠ ШәЩҒШұШӘ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ Щ…ЩҶЩғ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШЈШЁШ§Щ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҠШ§ Ш§ШЁЩҶ ШўШҜЩ… Щ„ЩҲ ШЁЩ„ШәШӘ Ш°ЩҶЩҲШЁЩғ Ш№ЩҶШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ШЎШҢ Ш«Щ… Ш§ШіШӘШәЩҒШұШӘЩҶЩҠ ШҢ ШәЩҒШұШӘ Щ„Щғ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШЈШЁШ§Щ„ЩҠШҢ ЩҠШ§ Ш§ШЁЩҶ ШўШҜЩ… ШҘЩҶЩғ Щ„ЩҲ ШЈШӘЩҠШӘЩҶЩҠ ШЁЩӮШұШ§ШЁ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ Ш®Ш·Ш§ЩҠШ§ШҢ Ш«Щ… Щ„ЩӮЩҠШӘЩҶЩҠ Щ„Ш§ ШӘШҙШұЩғ ШЁЩҠ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢШҢ Щ„ШЈШӘЩҠШӘЩғ ШЁЩӮШұШ§ШЁЩҮШ§ Щ…ШәЩҒШұШ©вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ (2)

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) said:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "O women folk! You should give charity and be diligent in seeking Allah's forgiveness because I have seen (i.e., on the Night of the Ascension to the highest heavens) that dwellers of the Hell are women." A woman amongst them said: "Why is it that the majority of the dwellers of Hell are women?" The Prophet (п·ә) replied, "You curse frequently and are ungrateful to your husbands. In spite of your lacking in wisdom and failing in religion, you are depriving the wisest of men of their intelligence." Upon this the woman asked: "What is the deficiency in our wisdom and in our religion?" He (п·ә) replied, "Your lack of wisdom can be well judged from the fact that the evidence of two women is equal to that one man. You do not offer Salat (prayer) for some days and you do not fast (the whole of) Ramadan sometimes, it is a deficiency in religion." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…Шұ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҠШ§ Щ…Ш№ШҙШұ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШіШ§ШЎ ШӘШөШҜЩӮЩҶШҢ ЩҲШЈЩғШ«ШұЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш§ШіШӘШәЩҒШ§ШұШҢ ЩҒШҘЩҶЩҠ ШұШЈЩҠШӘЩғЩҶ ШЈЩғШ«Шұ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШұвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘ Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШ© Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ ШЈЩғШ«Шұ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШұвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШӘЩғШ«ШұЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„Ш№ЩҶШҢ ЩҲШӘЩғЩҒШұЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш№ШҙЩҠШұ Щ…Ш§ШұШЈЩҠШӘ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҶШ§ЩӮШөШ§ШӘ Ш№ЩӮЩ„ ЩҲШҜЩҠЩҶ ШЈШәЩ„ШЁ Щ„Ш°ЩҠ Щ„ШЁ Щ…ЩҶЩғЩҶвҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ШӘвҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…Ш§ ЩҶЩӮШөШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш№ЩӮЩ„ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШҜЩҠЩҶвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҙЩҮШ§ШҜШ© Ш§Щ…ШұШЈШӘЩҠЩҶ ШЁШҙЩҮШ§ШҜШ© ШұШ¬Щ„ШҢ ЩҲШӘЩ…ЩғШ« Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҠШ§Щ… Щ„Ш§ ШӘШөЩ„ЩҠвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Jabir (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "The inhabitants of Jannah will eat and drink therein, but they will not have to pass excrement, to blow their noses or to urinate. Their food will be digested producing belch which will give out a smell like that of musk. They will be inspired to declare the freedom of Allah from imperfection and proclaim His Greatness as easily as you breathe." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш¬Ш§ШЁШұ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңЩҠШЈЩғЩ„ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ ШҢ ЩҲЩҠШҙШұШЁЩҲЩҶ ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШӘШәЩҲШ·ЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШӘЩ…Ш®Ш·ЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШЁЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩҶ Ш·Ш№Ш§Щ…ЩҮЩ… Ш°Щ„Щғ Ш¬ШҙШ§ШЎ ЩғШұШҙШӯ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩғШҢ ЩҠЩ„ЩҮЩ…ЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӘШіШЁЩҠШӯ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӘЩғШЁЩҠШұШҢ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ЩҠЩ„ЩҮЩ…ЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҒШівҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Allah, the Exalted, has said: 'I have prepared for my righteous slaves what no eye has seen, no ear has heard, and the mind of no man has conceived.' If you wish, recite: 'No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy as a reward for what they used to do."' (32:17) [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„Щү ШЈШ№ШҜШҜШӘ Щ„Ш№ШЁШ§ШҜЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШөШ§Щ„ШӯЩҠЩҶ Щ…Ш§ Щ„Ш§ Ш№ЩҠЩҶ ШұШЈШӘ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШЈШ°ЩҶ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ Ш®Ш·Шұ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩӮЩ„ШЁ ШЁШҙШұШҢ ЩҲШ§ЩӮШұШӨЩҲШ§ ШҘЩҶ ШҙШҰШӘЩ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘШ№Щ„Щ… ЩҶЩҒШі Щ…Ш§ ШЈШ®ЩҒЩҠ Щ„ЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩҶ ЩӮШұШ© ШЈШ№ЩҠЩҶ Ш¬ШІШ§ШЎ ШЁЩ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶЩҲШ§ ЩҠШ№Щ…Щ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ}вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШ§Щ„ШіШ¬ШҜШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ17вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "The first group (of people) to enter Jannah will be shining like the moon on a full-moon night. Then will come those who follow them who will be like the most shining planet in the sky. They will not stand in need of urinating or relieving of nature or of spitting or blowing their noses. Their combs will be of gold and their sweat will smell like musk; in their censers the aloes-wood will be used. Their wives will be large eyed maidens. All men will be alike in the form of their father 'Adam, sixty cubits tall." Another narration is: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Their utensils will be of gold, their perspiration will smell like musk; everyone of them will have two wives; the marrow of the bones of the wives' legs will be seen through the flesh out of excessive beauty. They (i.e., the people of Jannah) will neither have difference, nor enmity (hatred) amongst themselves; their hearts will be as if one heart, and they will be glorifying Allah in the morning and in the afternoon." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ вҖҸШЈЩҲЩ„ ШІЩ…ШұШ© ЩҠШҜШ®Щ„ЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Ш№Щ„Щү ШөЩҲШұШ© Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩ…Шұ Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш§Щ„ШЁШҜШұШҢ Ш«Щ… Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ЩҠЩ„ЩҲЩҶЩҮЩ… Ш№Щ„Щү ШЈШҙШҜ ЩғЩҲЩғШЁ ШҜШұЩҠ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ШЎ ШҘШ¶Ш§ШЎШ©ШҢ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШЁЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШӘШәЩҲШ·ЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШӘЩҒЩ„ЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШӘЩ…Ш®Ш·ЩҲЩҶШҢ ШЈЩ…ШҙШ§Ш·ЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҮШЁШҢ ЩҲШұЩҠШӯЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩғШҢ ЩҲЩ…Ш¬Ш§Щ…ШұЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШЈЩ„ЩҲШ© -Ш№ЩҲШҜ Ш§Щ„Ш·ЩҠШЁ- ШЈШІЩҲШ§Ш¬ЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШӯЩҲШұШ§Щ„Ш№ЩҠЩҶШҢ Ш№Щ„Щү Ш®Щ„ЩӮ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜШҢ Ш№Щ„Щү ШөЩҲШұШ© ШЈШЁЩҠЩҮЩ… ШўШҜЩ… ШіШӘЩҲЩҶ Ш°ШұШ§Ш№Ш§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ШЎвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© Щ„Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠ ЩҲЩ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШўЩҶЩҠШӘЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҮШЁШҢ ЩҲШұШҙШӯЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…ШіЩғШҢ ЩҲЩ„ЩғЩ„ ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜ Щ…ЩҶЩҮЩ… ШІЩҲШ¬ШӘШ§ЩҶ ЩҠШұЩү Щ…Ш® ШіШ§ЩӮЩҮЩ…Ш§ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҲШұШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„Щ„ШӯЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӯШіЩҶШҢ Щ„Ш§ Ш§Ш®ШӘЩ„Ш§ЩҒ ШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮЩ… ШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШӘШЁШ§ШәШ¶вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮЩ„ЩҲШЁЩҮЩ… ЩӮЩ„ШЁ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜШҢ ЩҠШіШЁШӯЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩғШұШ© ЩҲШ№ШҙЩҠШ§ЩӢвҖҸ. ЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҮ: (Ш№Щ„Щү Ш®Щ„ЩӮ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜ). ШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЁШ№Ш¶ЩҮЩ… ШЁЩҒШӘШӯ Ш§Щ„Ш®Ш§ШЎ ЩҲШ§ШіЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲШЁШ№Ш¶ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ¶Щ…ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩҲЩғЩ„Ш§ЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШөШӯЩҠШӯ.

hadiths.narrator: Al-Mughirah bin Shu'bah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Musa (Moses) (п·ә) asked his Rubb: 'Who amongst the inhabitants of Jannah will be the lowest in rank?' He said: 'It will be a person who will be admitted into Jannah last of all when all the dwellers of Jannah have entered Jannah. It will be said to him: Enter Jannah. But he will say: O my Rubb! How should I enter while the people have settled in their apartments and taken their shares? It will be said to him: Will you be satisfied and pleased if you have a kingdom like that of a monarch of the world? He will say: I will be content, my Rubb. Allah will say: For you is that, and like that and like that and like that and like that. He will say at the fifth time: I am well-pleased, my Rubb. Allah will say: It is for you and ten times more like it. You will have whatever your soul desires and whatever your eyes could delight in. He will say: I am well-pleased, my Rubb.' Musa (п·ә) said: 'Who will be of the highest rank in Jannah.' Allah said: 'They are those whom I chose and I established their honour with My Own Hand. I attest with My Seal that they will be blessed with such bounties as no eye has seen, no ear has heard and no human mind has perceived."' [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШәЩҠШұШ© ШЁЩҶ ШҙШ№ШЁШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШіШЈЩ„ Щ…ЩҲШіЩү ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШұШЁЩҮШҢ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШҜЩҶЩү ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Щ…ЩҶШІЩ„Ш© вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩҲ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҠШ¬ЩҠШЎ ШЁШ№ШҜ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШҜШ®Щ„ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©-Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ„ЩҮ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§ШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩҠ ШұШЁ ЩғЩҠЩҒ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ЩҶШІЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші Щ…ЩҶШ§ШІЩ„ЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲШЈШ®Ш°ЩҲШ§ ШЈШ®Ш°Ш§ШӘЩҮЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШӘШұШ¶Щү ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩғЩҲЩҶ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш«Щ„ Щ…Щ„Щғ Щ…Щ„Щғ Щ…ЩҶ Щ…Щ„ЩҲЩғ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҶЩҠШ§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШұШ¶ЩҠШӘ ШұШЁ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„Щғ Ш°Щ„Щғ ЩҲЩ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲЩ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш®Ш§Щ…ШіШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШұШ¶ЩҠШӘ ЩҠШ§ ШұШЁ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Щ„Щғ ЩҲШ№ШҙШұШ© ШЈЩ…Ш«Ш§Щ„ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щғ Щ…Ш§ Ш§ШҙШӘЩҮЩҠШӘ ЩҶЩҒШіЩғШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш°ШӘ Ш№ЩҠЩҶЩғШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШұШ¶ЩҠШӘ ШұШЁШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШЁ ЩҒШЈШ№Щ„Ш§ЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩҶШІЩ„Ш©вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩҲЩ„ШҰЩғ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ШЈШұШҜШӘШӣ ШәШұШіШӘ ЩғШұШ§Щ…ШӘЩҮЩ… ШЁЩҠШҜЩҠШҢ ЩҲШ®ШӘЩ…ШӘ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ШӘШұ Ш№ЩҠЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ШӘШіЩ…Ш№ ШЈШ°ЩҶШҢ ЩҲЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ®Ш·Шұ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩӮЩ„ШЁ ШЁШҙШұвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: 'Abdullah bin Mas'ud (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "I know of the last of the inhabitants of the Hell to be taken out from there and the last one to enter Jannah. He is a man who will come out of the Fire, crawling on all fours. Allah, the Rubb of glory and honour will say to him: 'Go and enter Jannah.' He will go to it and think that it is full up. He will then come back and say: 'O my Rubb, it is full up.' Allah will say to him: 'Go and enter Jannah.' He will again go to it and think that it is full up. So he will turn back. Allah will again say: 'Go and enter Jannah. For you have what is equal to ten times the world.' He will say: 'Are You making fun of me while You are the King?" At this I (i.e., the narrator) saw the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) laugh till his premolars were visible and he said, "Such man will be the last dweller of Jannah in its lowest rank." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Щ…ШіШ№ЩҲШҜ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶЩҠ Щ„ШЈШ№Щ„Щ… ШўШ®Шұ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Шұ Ш®ШұЩҲШ¬Ш§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲШўШ®Шұ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ШҜШ®ЩҲЩ„Ш§ЩӢ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ШұШ¬Щ„ ЩҠШ®ШұШ¬ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Шұ ШӯШЁЩҲШ§Шӣ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁ ЩҒШ§ШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШ®ЩҠЩ„ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШЈЩҶЩҮШ§ Щ…Щ„ШЈЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠШұШ¬Ш№ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШЁ ЩҲШ¬ШҜШӘЩҮШ§ Щ…Щ„ШЈЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁ ЩҒШ§ШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШұШ¬Ш№ ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШЁ ЩҲШ¬ШҜШӘЩҮШ§ Щ…Щ„ШЈЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩҲШұШ© ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ Щ„ЩҮ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁ ЩҒШ§ШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШЈШӘЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШ®ЩҠЩ„ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮ ШЈЩҶЩҮШ§ Щ…Щ„ШЈЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠШұШ¬Ш№ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШЁ ЩҲШ¬ШҜШӘЩҮШ§ Щ…Щ„ШЈЩүвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Ш°ЩҮШЁ ЩҒШ§ШҜШ®Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒШҘЩҶ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш«Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҶЩҠШ§ ЩҲШ№ШҙШұШ© ШЈЩ…Ш«Ш§Щ„ЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҲ ШҘЩҶ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш«Щ„ Ш№ШҙШұШ© ШЈЩ…Ш«Ш§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҶЩҠШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШӘШіШ®Шұ ШЁЩҠШҢ ШЈЩҲ ШӘШ¶ШӯЩғ ШЁЩҠ ЩҲШЈЩҶШӘ Ш§Щ„Щ…Щ„ЩғвҖқ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒЩ„ЩӮШҜ ШұШЈЩҠШӘ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш¶ШӯЩғ ШӯШӘЩү ШЁШҜШӘ ЩҶЩҲШ§Ш¬Ш°ЩҮ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШ°Щ„Щғ ШЈШҜЩҶЩү ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Щ…ЩҶШІЩ„Ш©вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Musa (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "In Jannah the believer will have a tent made of a single hollowed pearl of which the length will be sixty miles in the sky. The believer will have his wives with him and he will visit them and they will not be able to see one another." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Щ…ЩҲШіЩү ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶ Щ„Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Щ„Ш®ЩҠЩ…Ш© Щ…ЩҶ Щ„ШӨЩ„ШӨШ© ЩҲШ§ШӯШҜШ© Щ…Ш¬ЩҲЩҒШ© Ш·ЩҲЩ„ЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ШЎ ШіШӘЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЩҠЩ„Ш§ЩӢШҢ Щ„Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҠШ·ЩҲЩҒ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…ШӨЩ…ЩҶ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШұЩү ШЁШ№Ш¶ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ№Ш¶Ш§ЩӢШҢ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸВ«Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҠЩ„В» : ШіШӘШ© ШўЩ„Ш§ЩҒ Ш°ШұШ§Ш№

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "There is a tree in Jannah that is so huge that if a rider of a swift horse has to cover its distance from one end to another in one hundred years, he will not be able to do so." Another narration from Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) is: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "A rider will not be able to cross its (the tree's) shade even after travelling for one hundred years." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШҜШұЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Щ„ШҙШ¬ШұШ© ЩҠШіЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„ШұШ§ЩғШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҲШ§ШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш¶Щ…Шұ Ш§Щ„ШіШұЩҠШ№ Щ…Ш§ШҰШ© ШіЩҶШ© Щ…Ш§ ЩҠЩӮШ·Ш№ЩҮШ§ вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲШұЩҲЩҠШ§ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ вҖҸ"вҖҸШ§Щ„ШөШӯЩҠШӯЩҠЩҶвҖҸ"вҖҸ ШЈЩҠШ¶Ш§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩҠШіЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„ШұШ§ЩғШЁ ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩҲШұШ© ЩҒЩҠ ШёЩ„ЩҮШ§ ШіЩҶШ© Щ…Ш§ ЩҠЩӮШ·Ш№ЩҮШ§вҖҸ"вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The dwellers of Jannah will look at those in the upper abodes above them as you look at a shining star which remains in the eastern or western horizon; such will be the difference in superiority which some of them have over others." The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) was asked: "Will those be the dwellings of the Prophets which no one else will be able to reach?" He (п·ә) replied, "Yes, but by Him in Whose Hand my soul is! men who believed in Allah and acknowledged the truthfulness of the Messengers will reach them." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Щ„ЩҠШӘШұШ§ШЎЩҲЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШәШұЩҒ Щ…ЩҶ ЩҒЩҲЩӮЩҮЩ… ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШӘШұШ§ШЎЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩғЩҲЩғШЁ Ш§Щ„ШҜШұЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШәШ§ШЁШұ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҒЩӮ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШҙШұЩӮ ШЈЩҲ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШәШұШЁ Щ„ШӘЩҒШ§Ш¶Щ„ Щ…Щ„Ш§ ШЁЩҠЩҶЩҮЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ЩҲШ§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ШӘЩ„Щғ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШІЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШЁЩҠШ§ШЎ Щ„Ш§ ЩҠШЁЩ„ШәЩҮШ§ ШәЩҠШұЩҮЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШЁЩ„Щү ЩҲШ§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ ЩҶЩҒШіЩҠ ШЁЩҠШҜЩҮ ШұШ¬Ш§Щ„ ШўЩ…ЩҶЩҲШ§ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҲШөШҜЩӮЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ…ШұШіЩ„ЩҠЩҶвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "A space in Jannah equal to the distance between the middle and the end of a bow will be better than all that upon which the sun rises and sets." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩ„ЩӮШ§ШЁ ЩӮЩҲШі ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Ш®ЩҠШұ Щ…Щ…Ш§ ШӘШ·Щ„Ш№ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…Ші ШЈЩҲ ШӘШәШұШЁвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "In Jannah there is a market to which the people will come every Friday. The northern wind will blow and shower fragrance on their faces and clothes and, consequently, it will enhance their beauty and loveliness. They will then return to their wives who will also have increased in their beauty and loveliness, and their families will say to them: 'We swear by Allah that you have been increased in beauty and loveliness since leaving us.' Thereupon they will reply: 'We swear by Allah that you have also been increased in beauty and loveliness since we left you."' [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈЩҶШі ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ШіЩҲЩӮШ§ЩӢ ЩҠШЈШӘЩҲЩҶЩҮШ§ ЩғЩ„ Ш¬Щ…Ш№Ш©вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒШӘЩҮШЁ ШұЩҠШӯ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩ…Ш§Щ„ШҢ ЩҒШӘШӯШ«ЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲЩҮЩҮЩ… ЩҲШ«ЩҠШ§ШЁЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҒЩҠШІШҜШ§ШҜЩҲЩҶ ШӯШіЩҶШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШ¬Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢ ЩҒЩҠШұШ¬Ш№ЩҲЩҶ ШҘЩ„Щү ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҠЩҮЩ…ШҢ ЩҲЩӮШҜ Ш§ШІШҜШ§ШҜЩҲШ§ ШӯШіЩҶШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШ¬Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЈЩҮЩ„ЩҲЩҮЩ…вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„ЩӮШҜ Ш§ШІШҜШҜШӘЩ… ШӯШіЩҶШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШ¬Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШЈЩҶШӘЩ… ЩҲШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Щ„ЩӮШҜ Ш§ШІШҜШҜШӘЩ… ШЁШ№ШҜЩҶШ§ ШӯШіЩҶШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШ¬Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ЩӢвҖҸ!вҖҸвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "The dwellers of Jannah will see the upper abodes of Jannah as you see the stars in the sky." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШіЩҮЩ„ ШЁЩҶ ШіШ№ШҜ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© Щ„ЩҠШӘШұШ§ШЎЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШәШұЩҒ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШӘШӘШұШ§ШЎЩҲЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩғЩҲЩғШЁ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩ…Ш§ШЎвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Sahl bin Sa'd (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: I was in the company of the Prophet (п·ә). He gave a description of Jannah and concluded with these words, "There will be bounties which no eye has seen, no ear has heard and no human heart has ever perceived." He (п·ә) then recited this Verse: "Their sides forsake their beds, to invoke their Rubb in fear and hope, and they spend (in charity in Allah's Cause) out of what We have bestowed on them. No person knows what is kept hidden for them of joy..." (32:16,17) [Al-Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶЩҮ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҙЩҮШҜШӘ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Щ…Ш¬Щ„ШіШ§ЩӢ ЩҲШөЩҒ ЩҒЩҠЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ШӯШӘЩү Ш§ЩҶШӘЩҮЩүШҢ Ш«Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„ ЩҒЩҠ ШўШ®Шұ ШӯШҜЩҠШ«ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ Щ…Ш§Щ„Ш§ Ш№ЩҠЩҶ ШұШЈШӘШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШЈШ°ЩҶ ШіЩ…Ш№ШӘШҢ ЩҲЩ„Ш§ Ш®Ш·Шұ Ш№Щ„Щү ЩӮЩ„ШЁ ШЁШҙШұвҖҸ вҖҸ Ш«Щ… ЩӮШұШЈ вҖҸ{вҖҸШӘШӘШ¬Ш§ЩҒЩү Ш¬ЩҶЩҲШЁЩҮЩ… Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш¶Ш§Ш¬Ш№вҖҸ}вҖҸ ШҘЩ„Щү ЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘШ№Щ„Щ… ЩҶЩҒШі Щ…Ш§ ШЈШ®ЩҒЩҠ Щ„ЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩҶ ЩӮШұШ© ШЈШ№ЩҠЩҶвҖҸ}вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id and Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "When the dwellers of Jannah enter Jannah, an announcer will call: (You have a promise from Allah that) you will live therein and you will never die; you will stay healthy therein and you will never fall ill; you will stay young and you will never become old; you will be under a constant bliss and you will never feel miserable." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ ЩҲШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШҘШ°Ш§ ШҜШ®Щ„ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© - Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©- ЩҠЩҶШ§ШҜЩҠ Щ…ЩҶШ§ШҜвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶ Щ„ЩғЩ… ШЈЩҶ ШӘШӯЩҠЩҲШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩ…ЩҲШӘЩҲШ§ ШЈШЁШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ Щ„ЩғЩ… ШЈЩҶ ШӘШөШӯЩҲШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘШіЩӮЩ…ЩҲШ§ ШЈШЁШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ Щ„ЩғЩ… ШЈЩҶ ШӘШҙШЁЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘЩҮШұЩ…ЩҲШ§ ШЈШЁШҜШ§ЩӢШҢ ЩҲШҘЩҶ Щ„ЩғЩ… ШЈЩҶ ШӘЩҶШ№Щ…ЩҲШ§ШҢ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШӘШЁШЈШіЩҲШ§ ШЈШЁШҜШ§ЩӢвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "The lowest place of any of you in Jannah will be that Allah will tell him to express his wish. He will wish and wish again. Allah will then ask him: 'Have you expressed your wish?' He will answer: 'Yes, I have.' Allah will say: 'You will have what you have wished for and the like thereof along with it."' [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶ ШЈШҜЩҶЩү Щ…ЩӮШ№ШҜ ШЈШӯШҜЩғЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ШЈЩҶ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӘЩ…ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠШӘЩ…ЩҶЩү ЩҲЩҠШӘЩ…ЩҶЩүШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩ„ ШӘЩ…ЩҶЩҠШӘвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҶШ№Щ…ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҒШҘЩҶ Щ„Щғ Щ…Ш§ ШӘЩ…ЩҶЩҠШӘ ЩҲЩ…Ш«Щ„ЩҮ Щ…Ш№ЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri (May Allah be pleased with him) said:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Allah, the Rubb of honour and glory, will say to the inhabitants of Jannah: 'O inhabitants of Jannah!' They will respond: 'Here we are! At Your service, O our Rubb. All good is in Your Hand!' He will ask them: 'Are you pleased?' They will reply: 'Why should we not be pleased, O Rubb, when You have given us what You have not given to any of Your creatures?' Allah will say: 'Shall I not give you something better than that?' They will ask: 'O Rubb! What can be better than that?' Allah will say: 'I shall bestow My Pleasure upon you and I shall never be displeased with you."' [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШҜШұЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ШІ ЩҲШ¬Щ„ ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Щ„ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҠШ§ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©ШҢ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ„ШЁЩҠЩғ ШұШЁЩҶШ§ ЩҲШіШ№ШҜЩҠЩғШҢ ЩҲШ§Щ„Ш®ЩҠШұ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҠШҜЩҠЩғ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҮЩ„ ШұШ¶ЩҠШӘЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ Щ„ЩҶШ§ Щ„Ш§ ЩҶШұШ¶Щү ЩҠШ§ ШұШЁЩҶШ§ ЩҲЩӮШҜ ШЈШ№Ш·ЩҠШӘЩҶШ§ Щ…Ш§Щ„Щ… ШӘШ№Ш· ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш®Щ„ЩӮЩғвҖҸ!вҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ„Ш§ ШЈШ№Ш·ЩҠЩғЩ… ШЈЩҒШ¶Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ Ш°Щ„Щғ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩҲШЈЩҠ ШҙЩҠШЎ ШЈЩҒШ¶Щ„ Щ…ЩҶ Ш°Щ„ЩғвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШӯЩ„ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғЩ… ШұШ¶ЩҲШ§ЩҶЩҠШҢ ЩҒЩ„Ш§ ШЈШіШ®Ш· Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩғЩ… ШЁШ№ШҜЩҮ ШЈШЁШҜШ§ЩӢвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Jarir bin 'Abdullah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: We were sitting with the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) when he looked at the full moon and observed, "You shall see your Rubb in the Hereafter as you are seeing this moon; and you will not feel the slightest inconvenience in seeing Him." [Al-Bukhari and Muslim].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш¬ШұЩҠШұ ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғЩҶШ§ Ш№ЩҶШҜ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҒЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩ…Шұ Щ„ЩҠЩ„Ш© Ш§Щ„ШЁШҜШұШҢ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶЩғЩ… ШіШӘШұЩҲЩҶ ШұШЁЩғЩ… Ш№ЩҠШ§ЩҶШ§ЩӢ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШӘШұЩҲЩҶ ЩҮШ°Ш§ Ш§Щ„ЩӮЩ…ШұШҢ Щ„Ш§ ШӘШ¶Ш§Щ…ЩҲЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ ШұШӨЩҠШӘЩҮвҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Suhaib (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "When the inhabitants of Jannah enter Jannah, Allah, the Glorious and Exalted, will say to them: 'Do you wish me to give you anything more?' They will reply: 'Have You not made our faces bright? Have You not brought us into Jannah and delivered us from the Hell?' And Allah will remove the Veil. The (dwellers of Jannah) will feel that they have not been awarded anything dearer to them than looking at their Rubb." [Muslim]. Allah, the Exalted, says: "Verily, those who believe and do deeds of righteousness, their Rubb will guide them through their Faith; under them will flow rivers in the Gardens of Delight (Jannah). Their way of request therein will be Subhanaka Allahumma (Glory to You, O Allah!) and Salam (peace, safety from evil) will be their greetings therein (Jannah)! and the close of their request will be: Al-hamdu lillahi Rabbil-'Alamin [All the praises and thanks are to Allah, the Rubb of 'Alamin (mankind, jinn and all that exists)]." (10:9,10)
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШөЩҮЩҠШЁ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸШҘШ°Ш§ ШҜШ®Щ„ ШЈЩҮЩ„ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© -Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ©- ЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШЁШ§ШұЩғ ЩҲШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӘШұЩҠШҜЩҲЩҶ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢ ШЈШІЩҠШҜЩғЩ…вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҲЩҶвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈЩ„Щ… ШӘШЁЩҠШ¶ ЩҲШ¬ЩҲЩҮЩҶШ§вҖҸШҹвҖҸ ШЈЩ„Щ… ШӘШҜШ®Щ„ЩҶШ§ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҶШ© ЩҲШӘЩҶШ¬ЩҶШ§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§ШұвҖҸШҹвҖҸ ЩҒЩҠЩғШҙЩҒ Ш§Щ„ШӯШ¬Ш§ШЁШҢ ЩҒЩ…Ш§ ШЈШ№Ш·ЩҲ ШҙЩҠШҰШ§ЩӢ ШЈШӯШЁ ШҘЩ„ЩҠЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШёШұ ШҘЩ„Щү ШұШЁЩҮЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШӘШ№Ш§Щ„ЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ{вҖҸШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠЩҶ ШўЩ…ЩҶЩҲШ§ ЩҲШ№Щ…Щ„ЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„ШөШ§Щ„ШӯШ§ШӘ ЩҠЩҮШҜЩҠЩҮЩ… ШұШЁЩҮЩ… ШЁШҘЩҠЩ…Ш§ЩҶЩҮЩ… ШӘШ¬ШұЩҠ Щ…ЩҶ ШӘШӯШӘЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶЩҮШ§Шұ ЩҒЩҠ Ш¬ЩҶШ§ШӘ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ№ЩҠЩ…* ШҜШ№ЩҲШ§ЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ ШіШЁШӯШ§ЩҶЩғ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ЩҲШӘШӯЩҠШӘЩҮЩ… ЩҒЩҠЩҮШ§ ШіЩ„Ш§Щ… ЩҲШўШ®Шұ ШҜШ№ЩҲШ§ЩҮЩ… ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШұШЁ Ш§Щ„Ш№Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩҠЩҶвҖҸ}вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩҠЩҲЩҶШівҖҸ:вҖҸ9ШҢ10вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
Ш§Щ„ШӯЩ…ШҜ Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш§Щ„Ш°ЩҠ ЩҮШҜШ§ЩҶШ§ Щ„ЩҮШ°Ш§ ЩҲЩ…Ш§ ЩғЩҶШ§ Щ„ЩҶЩҮШӘШҜЩү Щ„ЩҲЩ„Ш§ ШЈЩҶ ЩҮШҜШ§ЩҶШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮвҖҸ.вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШөЩ„ Ш№Щ„Щү Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜ ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү ШўЩ„ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜШҢ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШөЩ„ЩҠШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү ШўЩ„ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШҢ ЩҲШЁШ§ШұЩғ Ш№Щ„Щү Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜШҢ ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү ШўЩ„ Щ…ШӯЩ…ШҜШҢ ЩғЩ…Ш§ ШЁШ§ШұЩғШӘ Ш№Щ„Щү ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ЩҲШ№Щ„Щү ШўЩ„ ШҘШЁШұШ§ЩҮЩҠЩ… ШҢ ШҘЩҶЩғ ШӯЩ…ЩҠШҜ Щ…Ш¬ЩҠШҜвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩӮШ§Щ„ Щ…ШӨЩ„ЩҒЩҮ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҒШұШәШӘ Щ…ЩҶЩҮ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш§Ш«ЩҶЩҠЩҶ ШұШ§ШЁШ№ ШҙЩҮШұ ШұЩ…Ш¶Ш§ЩҶ ШіЩҶШ© ШіШЁШ№ЩҠЩҶ ЩҲШіШӘЩ…Ш§ШҰШ©вҖҸ"вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ka'b bin Malik (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) set out on Thursday for the expedition of Tabuk. He liked to set out on a journey on Thursdays. [Al-Bukhari and Muslim]
hadiths.arabic:
Ш№ЩҶ ЩғШ№ШЁ ШЁЩҶ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩғШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ…Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Ш®ШұШ¬ ЩҒЩҠ ШәШІЩҲШ© ШӘШЁЩҲЩғ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ…ЩҠШіШҢ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҠШӯШЁ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШ®ШұШ¬ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ…ЩҠШівҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸЩ…ШӘЩҒЩӮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩҲЩҒЩҠ ШұЩҲШ§ЩҠШ© ЩҒЩҠ вҖңШ§Щ„ШөШӯЩҠШӯЩҠЩҶвҖқ Щ„ЩӮЩ„Щ…Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩҠШ®ШұШ¬ ШҘЩ„Ш§ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш§Щ„Ш®Щ…ЩҠШівҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Sakhr bin Wada'ah Al-Ghamidi (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "O Allah! Bless my people in the early part of the day (morning). Whenever he dispatched a detachment or an army-unit, he would dispatch it at the beginning of the day (soon after dawn). The narrator, Sakhr (May Allah be pleased with him) was a merchant, and he used to send off his merchandise at the beginning of the day. So his trade flourished and he made a good fortune. [At-Tirmidhi and Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШөШ®Шұ ШЁЩҶ ЩҲШҜШ§Ш№Ш© Ш§Щ„ШәШ§Щ…ШҜЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШөШӯШ§ШЁЩҠ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ШЈЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҮЩ… ШЁШ§ШұЩғ Щ„ШЈЩ…ШӘЩҠ ЩҒЩҠ ШЁЩғЩҲШұЩҮШ§вҖҸ"вҖҸ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ШҘШ°Ш§ ШЁШ№Ш« ШіШұЩҠШ© ШЈЩҲ Ш¬ЩҠШҙШ§ЩӢ ШЁШ№Ш«ЩҮЩ… Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҮШ§ШұвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ШөШ®Шұ ШӘШ§Ш¬ШұШ§ЩӢ ЩҒЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҠШЁШ№Ш« ШӘШ¬Ш§ШұШӘЩҮ ШЈЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҮШ§ШұШҢ ЩҒШЈШ«ШұЩҠ ЩҲЩғШ«Шұ Щ…Ш§Щ„ЩҮвҖқ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Umar (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Were people to know of what I know about the dangers of travelling alone, no rider would travel alone at night." [Al-Bukhari].
hadiths.arabic: Ш№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…Шұ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ"вҖҸЩ„ЩҲ ШЈЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҠШ№Щ„Щ…ЩҲЩҶ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҲШӯШҜШ© Щ…Ш§ ШЈШ№Щ„Щ… Щ…Ш§ ШіШ§Шұ ШұШ§ЩғШЁ ШЁЩ„ЩҠЩ„ ЩҲШӯШҜЩҮвҖқ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁШ®Ш§ШұЩҠвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: 'Amr bin Shu'aib (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "A single rider is (accompanied with) Satan and two riders are (accompanied with) two Satans. Three riders form a group." [At-Tirmidhi and Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш№Щ…Шұ ШЁЩҶ ШҙШ№ЩҠШЁ Ш№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠЩҮ Ш№ЩҶ Ш¬ШҜЩҮ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШ§Щ„ШұШ§ЩғШЁ ШҙЩҠШ·Ш§ЩҶ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШұШ§ЩғШЁШ§ЩҶ ШҙЩҠШ·Ш§ЩҶШ§ЩҶ ЩҲШ§Щ„Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© ШұЩғШЁвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠ ЩҲШ§Щ„ЩҶШіШ§ШҰЩҠ ШЁШЈШіШ§ЩҶЩҠШҜ ШөШӯЩҠШӯШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri and Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "When three persons set out on a journey, they should appoint one of them as their leader." [Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ШіШ№ЩҠШҜ ЩҲШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ ЩӮШ§Щ„Ш§вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШҘШ°Ш§ Ш®ШұШ¬ Ш«Щ„Ш§Ш«Ш© ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩҒШұ ЩҒЩ„ЩҠШЈЩ…ШұЩҲШ§ ШЈШӯШҜЩҮЩ…вҖҸ"вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶШҢ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ШЁШҘШіЩҶШ§ШҜ ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Ibn 'Abbas (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: The Prophet (п·ә) said, "The best number of companions is four; the best detachment is four hundred and the best army is four thousand; and twelve thousand men will not be defeated as a result of smallness of number." [At-Tirmidhi and Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ Ш§ШЁЩҶ Ш№ШЁШ§Ші ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ Ш№ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШЁЩҠ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШ®ЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„ШөШӯШ§ШЁШ© ШЈШұШЁШ№Ш© ЩҲШ®ЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„ШіШұШ§ЩҠШ§ ШЈШұШЁШ№Щ…Ш§ШҰШ© ЩҲШ®ЩҠШұ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ЩҠЩҲШҙ ШЈШұШЁШ№Ш© ШўЩ„Ш§ЩҒ ЩҲЩ„ЩҶ ЩҠШәЩ„ШЁ Ш§Ш«ЩҶШ§ Ш№ШҙШұ ШЈЩ„ЩҒШ§ЩӢ Ш№ЩҶ ЩӮЩ„Ш©вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШӘШұЩ…Ш°ЩҠ ЩҲЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШҜЩҠШ« ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Hurairah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said: "When you travel through green and lush land, give the camels their due from the ground, and when you travel through a dry and barren land, make them walk quickly lest their strength falters. When you encamp at night, keep away from the roads, for beasts pass there and they are the resort of insects at night." [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic:
Ш№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩҮШұЩҠШұШ© ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШҘШ°Ш§ ШіШ§ЩҒШұШӘЩ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш®ШөШЁ ЩҒШЈШ№Ш·ЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„ШҘШЁЩ„ ШӯШёЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ЩҲШҘШ°Ш§ ШіШ§ЩҒШұШӘЩ… ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Ш¬ШҜШЁ ЩҒШЈШіШұШ№ЩҲШ§ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮШ§ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҠШұ ЩҲШЁШ§ШҜШұЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҶЩӮЩҠЩҮШ§ШҢ ЩҲШҘШ°Ш§ Ш№ШұШіШӘЩ…ШҢ ЩҒШ§Ш¬ШӘЩҶШЁЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Ш·ШұЩҠЩӮШҢ ЩҒШҘЩҶ Ш·ШұЩӮ Ш§Щ„ШҜЩҲШ§ШЁШҢ ЩҲЩ…ШЈЩҲЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҮЩҲШ§Щ… ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„вҖқ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
Щ…Ш№ЩҶЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖңШЈШ№Ш·ЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„ШҘШЁЩ„ ШӯШёЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶вҖқ ШЈЩҠвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§ШұЩҒЩӮЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҮШ§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҠШұ Щ„ШӘШұШ№ЩҠ ЩҒЩҠ ШӯШ§Щ„ ШіЩҠШұЩҮШ§ ЩҲЩӮЩҲЩ„ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸЩҶЩӮЩҠЩҮШ§вҖҸ вҖҸ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ ШЁЩғШіШұ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҲЩҶШҢ ЩҲШҘШіЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩӮШ§ЩҒШҢ ЩҲШЁШ§Щ„ЩҠШ§ШЎ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш«ЩҶШ§Ш© Щ…ЩҶ ШӘШӯШӘ ЩҲЩҮЩҲвҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш®ШҢ Щ…Ш№ЩҶШ§ЩҮвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШіШұШ№ЩҲШ§ ШЁЩҮШ§ ШӯШӘЩҠ ШӘШөЩ„ЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ…ЩӮШөШҜ ЩӮШЁЩ„ ШЈЩҶ ЩҠШ°ЩҮШЁ Щ…Ш®ЩҮШ§ Щ…ЩҶ Ш¶ЩҶЩғ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҠШұвҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҲвҖҸ вҖҸШ§Щ„ШӘШ№ШұЩҠШівҖҸ вҖҸ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШІЩҲЩ„ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Qatadah (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: When the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) encamped at night during a journey, he would lie down on his right side, and when he stayed a little before dawn he would raise his forearm and place his head on the palm of his hand. [Muslim].
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ ЩӮШӘШ§ШҜШ©ШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҢ ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩғШ§ЩҶ ЩҒЩҠ ШіЩҒШұШҢ ЩҒШ№ШұШі ШЁЩ„ЩҠЩ„ Ш§Ш¶Ш·Ш¬Ш№ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ ЩҠЩ…ЩҠЩҶЩҮ ЩҲШҘШ°Ш§ Ш№ШұШі ЩӮШЁЩҠЩ„ Ш§Щ„ШөШЁШӯ ЩҶШөШЁ Ш°ШұШ§Ш№ЩҮШҢ ЩҲЩҲШ¶Ш№ ШұШЈШіЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠ ЩғЩҒЩҮвҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ Щ…ШіЩ„Щ…вҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
ЩӮШ§Щ„ Ш§Щ„Ш№Щ„Щ…Ш§ШЎвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ ЩҶШөШЁ Ш°ШұШ§Ш№ЩҮ Щ„ШҰЩ„Ш§ ЩҠШіШӘШәШұЩӮ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ЩҶЩҲЩ…ШҢ ЩҒШӘЩҒЩҲШӘЩҮ ШөЩ„Ш§Ш© Ш§Щ„ШөШЁШӯ Ш№ЩҶ ЩҲЩӮШӘЩҮШ§ ШЈЩҲ Ш№ЩҶ ШЈЩҲЩ„ ЩҲЩӮШӘЩҮШ§вҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Anas (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) said, "Keep to travelling by night because the earth is folded (traversed more easily) during the night." [Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic:
Ш№ЩҶ ШЈЩҶШіШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШ№Щ„ЩҠЩғЩ… ШЁШ§Щ„ШҜЩ„Ш¬Ш©ШҢ ЩҒШҘЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШЈШұШ¶ ШӘШ·ЩҲЩҠ ШЁШ§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ШЁШҘШіЩҶШ§ШҜ ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ
Ш§Щ„ШҜЩ„Ш¬Ш©вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ:вҖҸ Ш§Щ„ШіЩҠШұ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҠЩ„вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Tha'labah Al-Khushani (May Allah be pleased with him) reported:
hadiths.body: Whenever the people encamped during the journey, they would disperse in the mountain passes and valleys. (When) the Messenger of Allah (п·ә) noticed this he said, "Your act of scattering in the mountain passes and valleys is from Satan." Afterwards the Companions kept close together whenever they encamped. [Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Ш«Ш№Щ„ШЁШ© Ш§Щ„Ш®ШҙЩҶЩҠШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ЩғШ§ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ШҘШ°Ш§ ЩҶШІЩ„ЩҲШ§ Щ…ЩҶШІЩ„Ш§ЩӢ ШӘЩҒШұЩӮЩҲШ§ ЩҒЩҠ Ш§Щ„ШҙШ№Ш§ШЁ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШЈЩҲШҜЩҠШ©вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮШҢ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШҘЩҶ ШӘЩҒШұЩӮЩғЩ… ЩҒЩҠ ЩҮШ°ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШҙШ№Ш§ШЁ ЩҲШ§Щ„ШЈЩҲШҜЩҠШ© ШҘЩҶЩ…Ш§ Ш°Щ„ЩғЩ… Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШҙЩҠШ·Ш§ЩҶвҖҸ!вҖҸвҖҸ вҖҸ ЩҒЩ„Щ… ЩҠЩҶШІЩ„ЩҲШ§ ШЁШ№ШҜ Ш°Щ„Щғ Щ…ЩҶШІЩ„Ш§ ШҘЩ„Ш§ Ш§ЩҶШ¶Щ… ШЁШ№Ш¶ЩҮЩ… ШҘЩ„ЩҠ ШЁШ№Ш¶вҖҸ.вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ШЁШҘШіЩҶШ§ШҜ ШӯШіЩҶвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Sahl bin 'Amr known as Ibn Al-Hanzaliyyah reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) happened to pass by a camel whose belly was sticking to its back (because of hunger), whereupon he said, "Fear Allah in respect of these dumb (animals). Ride them while they are fit, and slaughter them and eat their meat when they are fit." [Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic: ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШіЩҮЩ„ ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…ШұЩҲ -ЩҲЩӮЩҠЩ„ ШіЩҮЩ„ ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШұШЁЩҠШ№ ШЁЩҶ Ш№Щ…ШұЩҲ Ш§Щ„ШЈЩҶШөШ§ШұЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№ШұЩҲЩҒ ШЁШ§ШЁЩҶ Ш§Щ„ШӯЩҶШёЩ„ЩҠШ©ШҢ ЩҲЩҮЩҲ Щ…ЩҶ ШЈЩҮЩ„ ШЁЩҠШ№Ш© Ш§Щ„ШұШ¶ЩҲШ§ЩҶШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ Щ…Шұ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШЁШЁШ№ЩҠШұ ЩӮШҜ Щ„ШӯЩӮ ШёЩҮШұЩҮ ШЁШ·ЩҶЩҮШҢ ЩҒЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸШ§ШӘЩӮЩҲШ§ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ЩҒЩҠ ЩҮШ°ЩҮ Ш§Щ„ШЁЩҮШ§ШҰЩҮЩ… Ш§Щ„Щ…Ш№Ш¬Щ…Ш©ШҢ ЩҒШ§ШұЩғШЁЩҲЩҮШ§ ШөШ§Щ„ШӯШ© ЩҲЩғЩ„ЩҲЩҮШ§ ШөШ§Щ„ШӯШ©вҖҸ вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ШЈШЁЩҲ ШҜШ§ЩҲШҜ ШЁШҘШіЩҶШ§ШҜ ШөШӯЩҠШӯвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ

hadiths.narrator: Abu Ja'far 'Abdullah bin Ja'far (May Allah be pleased with them) reported:
hadiths.body: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) made me ride behind him one day and confided something to me which I shall never disclose to anyone. ('Abdullah added:) He ((п·ә) preferred to screen himself from others when responding to the call of nature, either behind a wall or the trunk of a date-palm. [Muslim]. Al-Barqani added: The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) entered an orchid belonging to an Ansari and saw there a camel. When it saw him, it began to groan and its eyes shed tears. The Messenger of Allah (п·ә) approached it and patted it on the hump and the base of its head until it quieted down. Then he (п·ә) asked, "Who is the owner of this camel? To whom does it belong?" An Ansari youth stepped forward and said: "It is mine O Messenger of Allah!" He said, "Do you not fear Allah in respect of this beast which Allah has placed in your possession? This camel is complaining to me that you starve it and put it to toil." [Abu Dawud].
hadiths.arabic:
ЩҲШ№ЩҶ ШЈШЁЩҠ Ш¬Ш№ЩҒШұ Ш№ШЁШҜ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШЁЩҶ Ш¬Ш№ЩҒШұШҢ ШұШ¶ЩҠ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№ЩҶЩҮЩ…Ш§ШҢ ЩӮШ§Щ„вҖҸ:вҖҸ ШЈШұШҜЩҒЩҶЩҠ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… ШҢ Ш°Ш§ШӘ ЩҠЩҲЩ… Ш®Щ„ЩҒЩҮШҢ ЩҲШЈШіШұ ШҘЩ„ЩҠ ШӯШҜЩҠШ«Ш§ЩӢ Щ„Ш§ ШЈШӯШҜШ« ШЁЩҮ ШЈШӯШҜШ§ЩӢ Щ…ЩҶ Ш§Щ„ЩҶШ§Ші ЩҲЩғШ§ЩҶ ШЈШӯШЁ Щ…Ш§ ШЈШіШӘШӘШұ ШЁЩҮ ШұШіЩҲЩ„ Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ ШөЩ„Щү Ш§Щ„Щ„ЩҮ Ш№Щ„ЩҠЩҮ ЩҲШіЩ„Щ… Щ„ШӯШ§Ш¬ШӘЩҮ ЩҮШҜЩҒ ШЈЩҲ ШӯШ§ШҰШҙ ЩҶШ®Щ„вҖҸ.вҖҸ ЩҠШ№ЩҶЩүвҖҸ:вҖҸ ШӯШ§ШҰШ· ЩҶШ®Щ„вҖҸ"вҖҸ вҖҸ(вҖҸвҖҸ(вҖҸШұЩҲШ§ЩҮ ЩҮЩғШ°Ш§ Щ…Ш®ШӘШөШұШ§ЩӢвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ)вҖҸвҖҸ.вҖҸ
